1	"""M-D1 precision metrics + benchmark runner.
2	
3	Given an InductorProtocol implementation + a ValidationSet, run the
4	inductor against every case and aggregate five metrics:
5	
6	  precision: in-scope cases where match_score >= tau / total in-scope
7	    accepted decisions
8	  silent_disagreement_rate: in-scope cases accepted by inductor where
9	    match_score < tau / total in-scope (this is the dangerous case —
10	    inductor confidently produced a wrong contract)
11	  abstain_recall: cases the inductor correctly abstained on
12	    (ambiguous + out_of_scope where inductor abstained) / total
13	    cases that should abstain
14	  abstain_precision (Codex round-1 fix): correct abstentions /
15	    total abstentions — high precision means few in-scope cases
16	    are needlessly routed to operator review.
17	  operator_review_load: total abstains / total cases (the "human-
18	    review load" the system places on operators)
19	
20	Acceptance per M-D1:
21	  precision >= 0.8
22	  silent_disagreement_rate <= 0.05
23	  abstain_recall >= 0.95
24	  abstain_precision >= 0.80
25	  operator_review_load <= 0.30
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
31	from typing import Any, Literal, Protocol
32	
33	import yaml
34	
35	from src.polaris_graph.auto_induction.benchmark_loader import (
36	    ValidationCase,
37	    ValidationSet,
38	)
39	from src.polaris_graph.auto_induction.contract_compare import (
40	    ContractComparison,
41	    compare_contracts,
42	)
43	
44	
45	# Codex round-1 review fix: decision was an unvalidated str. Now a
46	# Literal so type-checkers + runtime validation enforce the contract.
47	DecisionLiteral = Literal["accept", "abstain"]
48	
49	
50	class InductorVerdictError(ValueError):
51	    """Raised when an InductorVerdict is malformed."""
52	
53	
54	@dataclass(frozen=True)
55	class InductorVerdict:
56	    """One inductor's response to one query.
57	
58	    `decision` is either:
59	      - "accept": inductor produced a contract (`induced_contract`
60	        non-None), implying it had high enough confidence.
61	      - "abstain": inductor declined to produce a contract; the
62	        runtime should fall back to operator review.
63	
64	    `is_terminal`: when True on an abstain, the verdict is FINAL
65	    — downstream wrappers (e.g. LLMAugmentedInductor) must NOT
66	    override it. Used by the keyword stub to signal disqualifier
67	    hits (semantically "I know this is out of scope") vs ordinary
68	    low-confidence abstains (where an LLM might legitimately
69	    have more information). Default False = override allowed.
70	
71	    Codex round-1 review fix: validate decision + enforce shape
72	    invariants. Previously any non-'abstain' string was treated as
73	    accept silently.
74	    """
75	
76	    decision: str
77	    induced_contract: Any | None = None
78	    confidence: float | None = None
79	    abstain_reason: str | None = None
80	    is_terminal: bool = False
81	
82	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
83	        if self.decision not in ("accept", "abstain"):
84	            raise InductorVerdictError(
85	                f"InductorVerdict.decision must be 'accept' or 'abstain', "
86	                f"got {self.decision!r}"
87	            )
88	        if self.decision == "accept" and self.induced_contract is None:
89	            raise InductorVerdictError(
90	                "InductorVerdict.decision='accept' requires non-None "
91	                "induced_contract"
92	            )
93	        if self.decision == "abstain" and self.induced_contract is not None:
94	            raise InductorVerdictError(
95	                "InductorVerdict.decision='abstain' must have None "
96	                "induced_contract (got an object)"
97	            )
98	        if self.confidence is not None and not (0.0 <= self.confidence <= 1.0):
99	            raise InductorVerdictError(
100	                f"InductorVerdict.confidence must be in [0,1] when set; "
101	                f"got {self.confidence}"
102	            )
103	
104	
105	class InductorProtocol(Protocol):
106	    """The inductor contract M-D2 will implement.
107	
108	    M-D1 ships only the harness; M-D2 will provide the actual
109	    implementation.
110	    """
111	
112	    def induce(self, query: str) -> InductorVerdict:
113	        ...
114	
115	
116	@dataclass(frozen=True)
117	class PrecisionMetrics:
118	    """Aggregated benchmark metrics.
119	
120	    Codex round-1 review fix: added abstain_precision to the metric
121	    pack. Phase D plan requires it; round-1 of M-D1 acceptance
122	    explicitly listed both abstain precision AND recall.
123	    """
124	
125	    total_cases: int
126	    in_scope_total: int
127	    in_scope_accepted: int
128	    in_scope_match_at_tau: int
129	    in_scope_silent_disagreements: int
130	    abstain_should_abstain_total: int
131	    abstain_correct: int
132	    abstain_total: int
133	
134	    @property
135	    def precision(self) -> float:
136	        if self.in_scope_accepted == 0:
137	            return 0.0
138	        return self.in_scope_match_at_tau / self.in_scope_accepted
139	
140	    @property
141	    def silent_disagreement_rate(self) -> float:
142	        if self.in_scope_total == 0:
143	            return 0.0
144	        return self.in_scope_silent_disagreements / self.in_scope_total
145	
146	    @property
147	    def abstain_recall(self) -> float:
148	        if self.abstain_should_abstain_total == 0:
149	            return 1.0  # vacuous
150	        return self.abstain_correct / self.abstain_should_abstain_total
151	
152	    @property
153	    def abstain_precision(self) -> float:
154	        """Of all abstentions, how many were correct?
155	
156	        Low abstain_precision means the inductor over-abstains —
157	        routing in-scope cases to operator review needlessly. The
158	        Phase D round-1 acceptance explicitly named abstain
159	        precision/recall as separate metrics; this implements the
160	        precision side.
161	        """
162	        if self.abstain_total == 0:
163	            return 1.0  # vacuous (no abstentions, all correct trivially)
164	        return self.abstain_correct / self.abstain_total
165	
166	    @property
167	    def operator_review_load(self) -> float:
168	        if self.total_cases == 0:
169	            return 0.0
170	        return self.abstain_total / self.total_cases
171	
172	    def passes_acceptance(
173	        self,
174	        *,
175	        precision_floor: float = 0.80,
176	        silent_disagreement_ceiling: float = 0.05,
177	        abstain_recall_floor: float = 0.95,
178	        abstain_precision_floor: float = 0.80,
179	        operator_review_ceiling: float = 0.30,
180	    ) -> bool:
181	        return (
182	            self.precision >= precision_floor
183	            and self.silent_disagreement_rate <= silent_disagreement_ceiling
184	            and self.abstain_recall >= abstain_recall_floor
185	            and self.abstain_precision >= abstain_precision_floor
186	            and self.operator_review_load <= operator_review_ceiling
187	        )
188	
189	
190	@dataclass(frozen=True)
191	class BenchmarkResult:
192	    """One full run of the benchmark."""
193	
194	    metrics: PrecisionMetrics
195	    case_results: tuple[
196	        tuple[ValidationCase, InductorVerdict, ContractComparison | None],
197	        ...,
198	    ] = field(default_factory=tuple)
199	
200	
201	def _load_curator_contract(slug: str) -> Any:
202	    """Load a curator-reviewed contract by slug. Lazy import to keep
203	    the auto_induction package decoupled from the V30 substrate when
204	    that substrate isn't on the import path.
205	
206	    Codex round-1 review fix: previously passed `Path` to
207	    `load_report_contract_for_slug` but the M-54 loader expects a
208	    parsed dict (see report_contract.py:181-201 — it returns None
209	    on non-dict template). Result: every in-scope case crashed.
210	    Fix: load the YAML to a dict first.
211	    """
212	    from pathlib import Path
213	
214	    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.report_contract import (
215	        load_report_contract_for_slug,
216	    )
217	
218	    config_root = (
219	        Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
220	        / "config" / "scope_templates"
221	    )
222	    # Try each known scope-template file; first hit wins.
223	    for domain_yaml in config_root.glob("*.yaml"):
224	        try:
225	            with domain_yaml.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as fp:
226	                template_dict = yaml.safe_load(fp)
227	        except Exception:
228	            continue
229	        contract = load_report_contract_for_slug(template_dict, slug)
230	        if contract is not None:
231	            return contract
232	    raise ValueError(
233	        f"curator contract slug not found in any template under "
234	        f"{config_root}: {slug!r}"
235	    )
236	
237	
238	def run_benchmark(
239	    inductor: InductorProtocol,
240	    validation_set: ValidationSet,
241	    *,
242	    tau: float = 0.80,
243	    confidence_threshold: float | None = None,
244	) -> BenchmarkResult:
245	    """Run an inductor against the validation set, aggregate metrics.
246	
247	    Codex round-1 review fix: confidence is now actionable. If
248	    `confidence_threshold` is set and the inductor's verdict has
249	    `confidence < threshold`, the verdict is treated as `abstain`
250	    even if `decision='accept'`. This makes M-D2 calibration
251	    sweepable: try threshold=0.5, 0.7, 0.9 and pick whichever
252	    minimizes silent-disagreement-rate without exceeding the
253	    operator-review-load ceiling.
254	    """
255	    case_results: list[
256	        tuple[ValidationCase, InductorVerdict, ContractComparison | None]
257	    ] = []
258	
259	    in_scope_total = len(validation_set.in_scope)
260	    in_scope_accepted = 0
261	    in_scope_match_at_tau = 0
262	    in_scope_silent_disagreements = 0
263	    abstain_should_abstain_total = (
264	        len(validation_set.ambiguous) + len(validation_set.out_of_scope)
265	    )
266	    abstain_correct = 0
267	    abstain_total = 0
268	
269	    for case in validation_set.all_cases:
270	        raw_verdict = inductor.induce(case.query)
271	
272	        # Codex round-1: confidence threshold downgrades accept to abstain.
273	        # Codex M-D2 round-1: store the EFFECTIVE verdict in
274	        # case_results when downgrading, so downstream consumers see
275	        # consistent (verdict.decision, comparison) tuples. Previously
276	        # case_results stored raw verdict (decision='accept') alongside
277	        # comparison=None for downgraded cases, which was confusing.
278	        effective_decision = raw_verdict.decision
279	        if (
280	            raw_verdict.decision == "accept"
281	            and confidence_threshold is not None
282	            and raw_verdict.confidence is not None
283	            and raw_verdict.confidence < confidence_threshold
284	        ):
285	            effective_decision = "abstain"
286	
287	        if effective_decision == "abstain":
288	            abstain_total += 1
289	            if case.expected_action == "abstain":
290	                abstain_correct += 1
291	            # If raw was accept but downgraded, build a synthetic
292	            # abstain verdict so case_results reflects what was
293	            # actually counted in the metrics.
294	            if raw_verdict.decision != "abstain":
295	                effective_verdict = InductorVerdict(
296	                    decision="abstain",
297	                    confidence=raw_verdict.confidence,
298	                    abstain_reason=(
299	                        f"confidence_threshold downgrade: "
300	                        f"{raw_verdict.confidence:.3f} < "
301	                        f"{confidence_threshold}"
302	                    ),
303	                )
304	            else:
305	                effective_verdict = raw_verdict
306	            case_results.append((case, effective_verdict, None))
307	            continue
308	
309	        # decision == "accept"
310	        if case.group == "in_scope":
311	            in_scope_accepted += 1
312	            assert case.curator_contract_slug is not None
313	            curator = _load_curator_contract(case.curator_contract_slug)
314	            cmp = compare_contracts(
315	                curator, raw_verdict.induced_contract,
316	            )
317	            if cmp.match_score >= tau:
318	                in_scope_match_at_tau += 1
319	            else:
320	                in_scope_silent_disagreements += 1
321	            case_results.append((case, raw_verdict, cmp))
322	        else:
323	            # Inductor accepted a case it should have abstained on.
324	            # No comparison meaningful (no curator contract for
325	            # ambiguous/out_of_scope).
326	            case_results.append((case, raw_verdict, None))
327	
328	    metrics = PrecisionMetrics(
329	        total_cases=validation_set.total,
330	        in_scope_total=in_scope_total,
331	        in_scope_accepted=in_scope_accepted,
332	        in_scope_match_at_tau=in_scope_match_at_tau,
333	        in_scope_silent_disagreements=in_scope_silent_disagreements,
334	        abstain_should_abstain_total=abstain_should_abstain_total,
335	        abstain_correct=abstain_correct,
336	        abstain_total=abstain_total,
337	    )
338	
339	    return BenchmarkResult(
340	        metrics=metrics,
341	        case_results=tuple(case_results),
342	    )

7	that aggregates per-system per-dimension scores.
8	
9	The Scoreboard is the artifact that report_renderer turns into HTML +
10	Markdown for the demo.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	from datetime import datetime, timezone
16	from typing import Any
17	
18	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
19	
20	from polaris_graph.benchmark.benchmark_config import (
21	    BenchmarkConfig,
22	    BenchmarkQuestion,
23	)
24	from polaris_graph.benchmark.dimension_scorers import (
25	    ALL_DIMENSIONS,
26	    DimensionName,
27	    DimensionScore,
28	    score_auditability,
29	    score_coverage_completeness,
30	    score_latency,
31	    score_numeric_grounding,
32	    score_provenance_density,
33	    score_refusal_correctness,
34	    score_sourcing_tier_mix,
35	)
36	from polaris_graph.benchmark.polaris_runner import PolarisRunResult
37	
38	
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	# Scoreboard schema
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	
43	class SystemScores(BaseModel):
44	    """One system's scores for a single question (one row in the scoreboard)."""
45	
46	    system: str  # 'polaris' | 'chatgpt' | 'gemini'
47	    by_dimension: dict[DimensionName, float | None]
48	    evidence: dict[DimensionName, list[str]] = Field(default_factory=dict)
49	
50	
51	class QuestionScores(BaseModel):
52	    """All systems' scores for one question."""
53	
54	    question_id: str
55	    question_text: str
56	    is_refusal_bait: bool
57	    polaris: SystemScores
58	    chatgpt: SystemScores
59	    gemini: SystemScores
60	
61	
62	class AggregateScoreboard(BaseModel):
63	    """Mean per-dimension per-system across all questions."""
64	
65	    polaris_mean: dict[DimensionName, float | None]
66	    chatgpt_mean: dict[DimensionName, float | None]
67	    gemini_mean: dict[DimensionName, float | None]
68	    n_questions: int
69	
70	
71	class Scoreboard(BaseModel):
72	    """Top-level benchmark output."""
73	
74	    benchmark_id: str
75	    ran_at_utc: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
76	    per_question: list[QuestionScores]
77	    aggregate: AggregateScoreboard
78	    polaris_wins: int          # dimensions where polaris > both externals (per-q)
79	    external_wins: int          # dimensions where some external > polaris (per-q)
80	    ties: int                   # dimensions where polaris == max external
81	
82	
83	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	# Per-question scoring
85	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	
87	def _polaris_dimension_scores(
88	    *,
89	    polaris: PolarisRunResult,
90	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
91	) -> dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore]:
92	    """Run all 7 scorers in 'POLARIS-side only' mode (external_text=None)."""
93	    pool = None
94	    if polaris.evidence_pool is not None:
95	        from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import EvidencePool
96	
97	        pool = EvidencePool.model_validate(polaris.evidence_pool)
98	    report = None
99	    if polaris.verified_report is not None:
100	        from polaris_graph.clinical_generator.verified_report import VerifiedReport
101	
102	        report = VerifiedReport.model_validate(polaris.verified_report)
103	
104	    scores: dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore] = {}
105	    scores["sourcing_tier_mix"] = score_sourcing_tier_mix(
106	        pool=pool, external_text=None, question=question
107	    )
108	    scores["numeric_grounding"] = score_numeric_grounding(
109	        report=report, pool=pool, external_text=None, question=question
110	    )
111	    scores["provenance_density"] = score_provenance_density(
112	        report=report, external_text=None, question=question
113	    )
114	    scores["refusal_correctness"] = score_refusal_correctness(
115	        report=report,
116	        polaris_intake_status=polaris.intake_status,
117	        external_text=None,
118	        question=question,
119	    )
120	    scores["coverage_completeness"] = score_coverage_completeness(
121	        report=report, external_text=None, question=question
122	    )
123	    scores["latency"] = score_latency(
124	        polaris_latency_ms=polaris.total_latency_ms or None,
125	        external_latency_ms=None,
126	        question=question,
127	    )
128	    scores["auditability"] = score_auditability(
129	        polaris_bundle_available=polaris.bundle_available,
130	        external_bundle_available=False,
131	        question=question,
132	    )
133	    return scores
134	
135	
136	def _external_dimension_scores(
137	    *,
138	    external_text: str | None,
139	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
140	) -> dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore]:
141	    """Run all 7 scorers in 'external-side only' mode."""
142	    scores: dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore] = {}
143	    scores["sourcing_tier_mix"] = score_sourcing_tier_mix(
144	        pool=None, external_text=external_text, question=question
145	    )
146	    scores["numeric_grounding"] = score_numeric_grounding(
147	        report=None, pool=None, external_text=external_text, question=question
148	    )
149	    scores["provenance_density"] = score_provenance_density(
150	        report=None, external_text=external_text, question=question
151	    )
152	    scores["refusal_correctness"] = score_refusal_correctness(
153	        report=None,
154	        polaris_intake_status=None,
155	        external_text=external_text,
156	        question=question,
157	    )
158	    scores["coverage_completeness"] = score_coverage_completeness(
159	        report=None, external_text=external_text, question=question
160	    )
161	    scores["latency"] = score_latency(
162	        polaris_latency_ms=None,
163	        external_latency_ms=None,  # external latencies typically not measured
164	        question=question,
165	    )
166	    scores["auditability"] = score_auditability(
167	        polaris_bundle_available=False,
168	        external_bundle_available=False,
169	        question=question,
170	    )
171	    return scores
172	
173	
174	def _to_system_scores(
175	    system: str,
176	    dim_scores: dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore],
177	    *,
178	    use_external: bool,
179	) -> SystemScores:
180	    """Convert dict[dim -> DimensionScore] to flat SystemScores."""
181	    by_dim: dict[DimensionName, float | None] = {}
182	    evidence: dict[DimensionName, list[str]] = {}
183	    for dim, score in dim_scores.items():
184	        if use_external:
185	            by_dim[dim] = score.external_score
186	            evidence[dim] = list(score.external_evidence)
187	        else:
188	            by_dim[dim] = score.polaris_score
189	            evidence[dim] = list(score.polaris_evidence)
190	    return SystemScores(system=system, by_dimension=by_dim, evidence=evidence)
191	
192	
193	def score_question(
194	    *,
195	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
196	    polaris: PolarisRunResult,
197	    chatgpt_text: str | None,
198	    gemini_text: str | None,
199	) -> QuestionScores:
200	    """Score one question across all 3 systems × 7 dimensions."""
201	    polaris_dim_scores = _polaris_dimension_scores(
202	        polaris=polaris, question=question
203	    )
204	    chatgpt_dim_scores = _external_dimension_scores(
205	        external_text=chatgpt_text, question=question
206	    )
207	    gemini_dim_scores = _external_dimension_scores(
208	        external_text=gemini_text, question=question
209	    )
210	
211	    return QuestionScores(
212	        question_id=question.question_id,
213	        question_text=question.question_text,
214	        is_refusal_bait=question.is_refusal_bait,
215	        polaris=_to_system_scores("polaris", polaris_dim_scores, use_external=False),
216	        chatgpt=_to_system_scores("chatgpt", chatgpt_dim_scores, use_external=True),
217	        gemini=_to_system_scores("gemini", gemini_dim_scores, use_external=True),
218	    )
219	
220	
221	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
222	# Aggregation
223	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
224	
225	def _mean_or_none(values: list[float | None]) -> float | None:
226	    """Mean of non-None values; None when all are None."""
227	    populated = [v for v in values if v is not None]
228	    if not populated:
229	        return None
230	    return sum(populated) / len(populated)
231	
232	
233	def _aggregate(per_question: list[QuestionScores]) -> AggregateScoreboard:
234	    """Per-system per-dimension means across all questions."""
235	
236	    def _means_for(system_attr: str) -> dict[DimensionName, float | None]:
237	        out: dict[DimensionName, float | None] = {}
238	        for dim in ALL_DIMENSIONS:
239	            values = [
240	                getattr(q, system_attr).by_dimension.get(dim)
241	                for q in per_question
242	            ]
243	            out[dim] = _mean_or_none(values)
244	        return out
245	
246	    return AggregateScoreboard(
247	        polaris_mean=_means_for("polaris"),
248	        chatgpt_mean=_means_for("chatgpt"),
249	        gemini_mean=_means_for("gemini"),
250	        n_questions=len(per_question),
251	    )
252	
253	
254	def _count_wins(per_question: list[QuestionScores]) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
255	    """Per question, per dimension: count polaris vs max(external) outcomes."""
256	    polaris_wins = 0
257	    external_wins = 0
258	    ties = 0
259	    for q in per_question:
260	        for dim in ALL_DIMENSIONS:
261	            p = q.polaris.by_dimension.get(dim)
262	            c = q.chatgpt.by_dimension.get(dim)
263	            g = q.gemini.by_dimension.get(dim)
264	            externals = [v for v in (c, g) if v is not None]
265	            if p is None and not externals:
266	                continue
267	            if p is None:
268	                external_wins += 1
269	                continue
270	            if not externals:
271	                polaris_wins += 1
272	                continue
273	            best_external = max(externals)
274	            if p > best_external:
275	                polaris_wins += 1
276	            elif p < best_external:
277	                external_wins += 1
278	            else:
279	                ties += 1
280	    return polaris_wins, external_wins, ties
281	
282	
283	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
284	# Public API
285	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	
287	def run_benchmark(
288	    *,
289	    config: BenchmarkConfig,
290	    polaris_results: dict[str, PolarisRunResult],
291	    chatgpt_outputs: dict[str, str],
292	    gemini_outputs: dict[str, str],
293	) -> Scoreboard:
294	    """Score every benchmark question across the 3 systems × 7 dimensions."""
295	    per_question: list[QuestionScores] = []
296	    for question in config.questions:
297	        polaris = polaris_results.get(question.question_id)
298	        if polaris is None:
299	            polaris = PolarisRunResult(
300	                question_id=question.question_id,
301	                failure="no PolarisRunResult provided",
302	            )
303	        per_question.append(
304	            score_question(
305	                question=question,
306	                polaris=polaris,
307	                chatgpt_text=chatgpt_outputs.get(question.question_id),
308	                gemini_text=gemini_outputs.get(question.question_id),
309	            )
310	        )
311	
312	    polaris_wins, external_wins, ties = _count_wins(per_question)
313	
314	    return Scoreboard(
315	        benchmark_id=config.benchmark_id,
316	        per_question=per_question,
317	        aggregate=_aggregate(per_question),
318	        polaris_wins=polaris_wins,
319	        external_wins=external_wins,
320	        ties=ties,
321	    )
322

content.text: File name "beat_both_scorer.py" and variable/module names "polaris_wins", "external_wins", "BEAT-BOTH scorer" — this is informal/marketing-flavored naming ("beat both" competitors) that reads unprofessionally in a code review, especially referencing named competitors (chatgpt, gemini) directly as system identifiers in comparison scoring code. This looks like an internal codename that leaked into production module naming. Flagging file name and the "beat_both" concept.

1	"""Benchmark configuration loader for slice 005 BEAT-BOTH.
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"benchmark_config".
4	
5	Loads a fixture file like config/benchmark/clinical_n10.json containing
6	N benchmark questions plus per-question expected metadata used for
7	scoring (which PICO axes should be covered, whether the question is
8	refusal-bait, etc.).
9	
10	The config is the contract between the architecture proposal's claims
11	and what `scripts/run_benchmark.py` actually exercises.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import json
17	from pathlib import Path
18	from typing import Literal
19	
20	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
21	
22	
23	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	# Type literals (mirror slice 001 / 003)
25	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	ScopeClassValue = Literal[
28	    "clinical_efficacy",
29	    "clinical_safety",
30	    "clinical_diagnosis",
31	    "clinical_prognosis",
32	    "out_of_scope",
33	]
34	
35	PicoAxisName = Literal["population", "intervention", "outcome"]
36	
37	
38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	# BenchmarkQuestion
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	
42	class BenchmarkQuestion(BaseModel):
43	    """One benchmark question with expected metadata."""
44	
45	    question_id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=100)
46	    question_text: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=2000)
47	    scope_class: ScopeClassValue
48	    expected_pico_axes: list[PicoAxisName] = Field(
49	        default_factory=list,
50	        description=(
51	            "PICO axes that should be covered in the response for "
52	            "coverage_completeness scoring."
53	        ),
54	    )
55	    expected_pico_keywords: list[str] = Field(
56	        default_factory=list,
57	        description=(
58	            "Optional content keywords (lowercase substrings) checked "
59	            "against verified prose for coverage_completeness. When "
60	            "present, takes precedence over the literal axis names — "
61	            "e.g. ['adults', 'aspirin', 'migraine'] for an aspirin/migraine "
62	            "question. Empty list = scorer falls back to expected_pico_axes."
63	        ),
64	    )
65	    is_refusal_bait: bool = Field(
66	        default=False,
67	        description=(
68	            "True for instruction-override or out-of-domain questions. "
69	            "POLARIS auto-scores 1.0 if it correctly refuses; external "
70	            "systems scored by content heuristic."
71	        ),
72	    )
73	    notes: str = Field(
74	        default="",
75	        description="Free-form context for evaluators (not scored).",
76	    )
77	
78	    @field_validator("question_id")
79	    @classmethod
80	    def _id_no_path_chars(cls, v: str) -> str:
81	        v = v.strip()
82	        if "/" in v or "\\" in v or ".." in v:
83	            raise ValueError(
84	                "question_id must not contain path separators or '..'"
85	            )
86	        return v
87	
88	    @field_validator("expected_pico_axes")
89	    @classmethod
90	    def _axes_unique(cls, v: list[str]) -> list[str]:
91	        if len(set(v)) != len(v):
92	            raise ValueError("expected_pico_axes must be unique")
93	        return v
94	
95	
96	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
97	# BenchmarkConfig
98	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99	
100	class BenchmarkConfig(BaseModel):
101	    """Top-level config for one benchmark run."""
102	
103	    benchmark_id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=100)
104	    benchmark_version: str = Field(default="1.0", max_length=20)
105	    description: str = Field(default="", max_length=1000)
106	    questions: list[BenchmarkQuestion] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
107	
108	    @field_validator("benchmark_id")
109	    @classmethod
110	    def _id_no_path_chars(cls, v: str) -> str:
111	        v = v.strip()
112	        if "/" in v or "\\" in v or ".." in v:
113	            raise ValueError("benchmark_id must not contain path separators")
114	        return v
115	
116	    @field_validator("questions")
117	    @classmethod
118	    def _question_ids_unique(
119	        cls, v: list[BenchmarkQuestion]
120	    ) -> list[BenchmarkQuestion]:
121	        ids = [q.question_id for q in v]
122	        if len(set(ids)) != len(ids):
123	            duplicates = sorted({i for i in ids if ids.count(i) > 1})
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                f"benchmark questions must have unique question_id; "
126	                f"duplicates: {duplicates}"
127	            )
128	        return v
129	
130	    def question_ids(self) -> list[str]:
131	        return [q.question_id for q in self.questions]
132	
133	    def question_by_id(self, question_id: str) -> BenchmarkQuestion | None:
134	        for q in self.questions:
135	            if q.question_id == question_id:
136	                return q
137	        return None
138	
139	    def refusal_bait_count(self) -> int:
140	        return sum(1 for q in self.questions if q.is_refusal_bait)
141	
142	
143	def load_config(path: Path) -> BenchmarkConfig:
144	    """Load + validate a benchmark config from a JSON file."""
145	    with Path(path).open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
146	        data = json.load(f)
147	    return BenchmarkConfig.model_validate(data)

1	"""I-meta-006 (#1006) — head-to-head FACT scorecard (lane1 faithfulness).
2	
3	Aggregates per-(system × question) ClaimRows into a per-system scorecard, split
4	into the clinical-3 (#75/#76/#78) and the overall-5 slices SEPARATELY (the locked
5	label; Codex design-gate APPROVE iter3). It REUSES ``claim_audit_scorer``.
6	
7	Lane2 (coverage) is GATED on the 5 hash-pinned gold rubrics, which do NOT exist
8	yet — authoring them is a SEPARATE follow-up Issue. While rubrics are pending the
9	scorecard NEVER calls the PASS rule (an empty rubric would read as coverage 0.00 →
10	a false fail, iter-2 P1). It reports **lane1 FACT faithfulness only**, surfaces
11	``lane2_pending: true`` + ``pass: null``, and makes NO PASS / "wins" claim.
12	``pass`` becomes a real boolean only once the hash-pinned rubrics land and
13	``score_system_question`` is given a non-empty rubric.
14	
15	Honest framing: per-claim-traceable, every number rolls up from the per-claim
16	ledger; NOT a one-number superiority headline.
17	"""
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import re
21	from collections import Counter
22	
23	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer import (
24	    ClaimRow,
25	    RubricElement,
26	    lane1_faithfulness,
27	    system_passes_question,
28	)
29	
30	# The locked 5-question slice (DRB-EN). Clinical = the 3 clinical questions.
31	CLINICAL_QIDS = ("75", "76", "78")
32	SOURCE_CRITICAL_QIDS = ("72", "90")
33	ALL_QIDS = ("75", "76", "78", "72", "90")
34	
35	
36	def normalize_qid(qid: str) -> str:
37	    """`Q75` / `DRB-EN#75` / `75` → `75`."""
38	    m = re.search(r"(\d{1,4})", str(qid))
39	    return m.group(1) if m else str(qid)
40	
41	
42	def score_system_question(
43	    rows: list[ClaimRow],
44	    *,
45	    rubric: list[RubricElement] | None = None,
46	) -> dict:
47	    """One (system × question) result. With NO rubric → lane1 FACT only +
48	    lane2_pending + pass=null (PASS rule withheld). With a rubric → the full
49	    PASS rule."""
50	    if not rubric:
51	        return {
52	            "lane1": lane1_faithfulness(rows),
53	            "lane2_pending": True,
54	            "pass": None,
55	        }
56	    result = system_passes_question(rows, rubric)
57	    return {
58	        "lane1": result["lane1"],
59	        "lane2": result["lane2"],
60	        "lane2_pending": False,
61	        "pass": result["passed"],
62	        "reasons": result["reasons"],
63	    }
64	
65	
66	def _aggregate_subset(
67	    rows_by_qid: dict[str, list[ClaimRow]], qids: tuple[str, ...],
68	) -> dict:
69	    rates: list[float] = []
70	    hard_fails = 0
71	    material = 0
72	    counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
73	    questions_present: list[str] = []
74	    for qid, rows in rows_by_qid.items():
75	        if qid not in qids:
76	            continue
77	        questions_present.append(qid)
78	        l1 = lane1_faithfulness(rows)
79	        rates.append(l1["unsupported_or_worse_rate"])
80	        hard_fails += l1["hard_fail_count"]
81	        material += l1["material_atoms"]
82	        for verdict, c in l1["verdict_counts"].items():
83	            counts[verdict] += c
84	    return {
85	        "questions_scored": sorted(questions_present),
86	        "n_questions": len(questions_present),
87	        "material_atoms": material,
88	        "mean_unsupported_or_worse_rate": (sum(rates) / len(rates)) if rates else 0.0,
89	        "hard_fail_count": hard_fails,
90	        "verdict_counts": dict(counts),
91	    }
92	
93	
94	def build_scorecard(
95	    rows_by_system_qid: dict[tuple[str, str], list[ClaimRow]],
96	    *,
97	    rubrics: dict[tuple[str, str], list[RubricElement]] | None = None,
98	    extended: dict | None = None,
99	) -> dict:
100	    """Per-system scorecard with clinical-3 + overall-5 slices.
101	
102	    ``rows_by_system_qid`` maps (system, question_id) → ClaimRows. ``rubrics`` is
103	    optional; when absent (the current state), lane2 is reported as pending and no
104	    PASS/"wins" claim is made. ``extended`` (I-perm-024 #1216) is an OPTIONAL
105	    precomputed extended-metrics block; when None (the DEFAULT) the returned dict is
106	    byte-identical to the pre-#1216 scorecard — the extended path is purely additive.
107	    """
108	    rubrics = rubrics or {}
109	    lane2_pending = len(rubrics) == 0
110	
111	    by_system: dict[str, dict[str, list[ClaimRow]]] = {}
112	    for (system, qid), rows in rows_by_system_qid.items():
113	        by_system.setdefault(system, {})[normalize_qid(qid)] = rows
114	
115	    systems: dict[str, dict] = {}
116	    for system, rows_by_qid in by_system.items():
117	        systems[system] = {
118	            "overall_5": _aggregate_subset(rows_by_qid, ALL_QIDS),
119	            "clinical_3": _aggregate_subset(rows_by_qid, CLINICAL_QIDS),
120	            "source_critical_2": _aggregate_subset(rows_by_qid, SOURCE_CRITICAL_QIDS),
121	        }
122	
123	    card = {
124	        "systems": systems,
125	        "lane2_pending": lane2_pending,
126	        "note": (
127	            "lane1 FACT faithfulness only; lane2 coverage pending hash-pinned gold "
128	            "rubrics (follow-up Issue); clinical-3 and overall-5 reported separately; "
129	            "per-claim traceable; NOT a superiority claim."
130	            if lane2_pending else
131	            "lane1 FACT faithfulness + lane2 coverage; per-claim traceable."
132	        ),
133	    }
134	    if extended is not None:
135	        card["extended"] = extended
136	    return card
137

1	"""Two-lane claim-audit scorer for the DR head-to-head (I-safety-002b / #925).
2	
3	REPLACES the §-1.1-banned/rigged `beat_both_scorer.py` + `dimension_scorers.py` (count /
4	pattern / string-match scoring + POLARIS auto-win dimensions). This scorer is a PURE
5	AGGREGATOR over the reconciled per-claim audit LEDGER produced by the Claude+Codex dual
6	§-1.1 line-by-line audit (claim → fetched cited span → verdict). It assigns NO verdicts
7	itself and gives POLARIS no free pass — every system's ledger is scored identically.
8	
9	Lane 1 = claim faithfulness (against the FETCHED cited span).
10	Lane 2 = pre-registered gold-rubric coverage (independent sources), each covered point
11	         itself citation-supported — so a terse report cannot win on faithfulness alone.
12	
13	Pure logic; fixture-tested with no model. Per plan `.codex/I-safety-002b/execution_plan_pathB.md`
14	§5/§6 (Codex APPROVE iter 5).
15	"""
16	
17	from __future__ import annotations
18	
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
20	from typing import Literal
21	
22	Verdict = Literal["VERIFIED", "PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE"]
23	Severity = Literal["S0", "S1", "S2", "S3"]
24	UnreachableSubtype = Literal[
25	    "paywall", "robots", "fetch_failure", "source_missing", "metadata_only",
26	]
27	
28	# Material = decision-relevant (S0-S2). S3 is observe-only, NOT scored (plan §5).
29	_MATERIAL_SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2")
30	# "unsupported-or-worse" verdicts that count against faithfulness.
31	_BAD_VERDICTS = ("PARTIAL", "UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE")
32	_PARTIAL_WEIGHT = 0.5  # pre-registered (plan §5)
33	_COVERAGE_THRESHOLD = 0.70  # literal, frozen (plan §5)
34	
35	
36	@dataclass
37	class ClaimRow:
38	    """One reconciled audit verdict for one atomic claim (from the dual line-by-line)."""
39	
40	    claim_id: str
41	    severity: Severity
42	    verdict: Verdict
43	    citation_id: str | None        # the citation the claim attaches to (None = uncited)
44	    span_quote: str | None         # exact supporting/refuting span from the FETCHED source
45	    unreachable_subtype: UnreachableSubtype | None = None
46	    audit_note: str | None = None  # e.g. "no supporting span found in cited source" for UNSUPPORTED
47	
48	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
49	        if self.verdict == "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is None:
50	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: UNREACHABLE requires an unreachable_subtype")
51	        if self.verdict != "UNREACHABLE" and self.unreachable_subtype is not None:
52	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: unreachable_subtype only valid for UNREACHABLE")
53	        # FABRICATED/PARTIAL must quote the refuting/partial span.
54	        if self.verdict in ("FABRICATED", "PARTIAL") and not self.span_quote:
55	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: {self.verdict} requires a span_quote (evidence)")
56	        # UNSUPPORTED on a CITED claim must record WHY (span checked or explicit no-support note)
57	        # so the verdict is traceable, not asserted (Codex P2).
58	        if self.verdict == "UNSUPPORTED" and self.citation_id and not (self.span_quote or self.audit_note):
59	            raise ValueError(f"{self.claim_id}: UNSUPPORTED+cited requires a span_quote or audit_note (traceability)")
60	
61	    @property
62	    def is_material(self) -> bool:
63	        return self.severity in _MATERIAL_SEVERITIES
64	
65	
66	@dataclass
67	class RubricElement:
68	    """One pre-registered gold-rubric required element (from independent sources)."""
69	
70	    element_id: str
71	    covered: bool             # did the report cover this required element?
72	    citation_supported: bool  # AND is the covered point itself citation-supported?
73	
74	
75	def lane1_faithfulness(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
76	    """S0-S2 material unsupported-or-worse rate + per-verdict breakdown. Positive = bad."""
77	    material = [r for r in rows if r.is_material]
78	    denom = len(material)
79	    counts: dict[str, int] = {v: 0 for v in ("VERIFIED", *_BAD_VERDICTS)}
80	    for r in material:
81	        counts[r.verdict] += 1
82	    # PARTIAL weighted 0.5; UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE full weight.
83	    weighted_bad = (
84	        counts["UNSUPPORTED"] + counts["FABRICATED"] + counts["UNREACHABLE"]
85	        + _PARTIAL_WEIGHT * counts["PARTIAL"]
86	    )
87	    rate = (weighted_bad / denom) if denom else 0.0
88	    return {
89	        "material_atoms": denom,
90	        "verdict_counts": counts,
91	        "weighted_unsupported_or_worse": weighted_bad,
92	        "unsupported_or_worse_rate": rate,
93	        "hard_fail_count": counts["UNSUPPORTED"] + counts["FABRICATED"],  # the gating verdicts
94	        "observed_S3_excluded": sum(1 for r in rows if r.severity == "S3"),
95	    }
96	
97	
98	def lane2_coverage(rubric: list[RubricElement]) -> dict:
99	    """Covered-AND-citation-supported / total required elements (plan §5/§6)."""
100	    total = len(rubric)
101	    covered_supported = sum(1 for e in rubric if e.covered and e.citation_supported)
102	    covered_only = sum(1 for e in rubric if e.covered)
103	    frac = (covered_supported / total) if total else 0.0
104	    return {
105	        "total_required": total,
106	        "covered": covered_only,
107	        "covered_and_citation_supported": covered_supported,
108	        "coverage_fraction": frac,
109	        "missing": [e.element_id for e in rubric if not (e.covered and e.citation_supported)],
110	    }
111	
112	
113	def system_passes_question(rows: list[ClaimRow], rubric: list[RubricElement]) -> dict:
114	    """A system PASSES a question iff zero S0-S2 FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED material claims AND
115	    coverage >= 0.70 (plan §5). Returns the verdict + the reasons (every gate explicit)."""
116	    l1 = lane1_faithfulness(rows)
117	    l2 = lane2_coverage(rubric)
118	    zero_hard_fail = l1["hard_fail_count"] == 0
119	    meets_coverage = l2["coverage_fraction"] >= _COVERAGE_THRESHOLD
120	    passed = zero_hard_fail and meets_coverage
121	    reasons = []
122	    if not zero_hard_fail:
123	        reasons.append(f"{l1['hard_fail_count']} S0-S2 FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED material claim(s)")
124	    if not meets_coverage:
125	        reasons.append(f"coverage {l2['coverage_fraction']:.2f} < {_COVERAGE_THRESHOLD}")
126	    return {"passed": passed, "reasons": reasons, "lane1": l1, "lane2": l2}
127	
128	
129	@dataclass
130	class SystemQuestionLedger:
131	    """The scored audit ledger for one (system × question). NOT a 'BEAT-BOTH' scoreboard."""
132	
133	    system: str         # "polaris" | "chatgpt" | "gemini" | "perplexity"
134	    question_id: str
135	    rows: list[ClaimRow]
136	    rubric: list[RubricElement]
137	    result: dict = field(init=False)
138	
139	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
140	        self.result = system_passes_question(self.rows, self.rubric)
141	
142	
143	def aggregate(ledgers: list[SystemQuestionLedger]) -> dict:
144	    """Per-system aggregate across questions. Every number traces to claim/span evidence in
145	    the per-(system×question) ledgers; NO cross-system 'wins' headline (plan §8)."""
146	    by_system: dict[str, dict] = {}
147	    for lg in ledgers:
148	        s = by_system.setdefault(lg.system, {"questions": 0, "passed": 0, "rates": [], "hard_fails": 0})
149	        s["questions"] += 1
150	        s["passed"] += int(lg.result["passed"])
151	        s["rates"].append(lg.result["lane1"]["unsupported_or_worse_rate"])
152	        s["hard_fails"] += lg.result["lane1"]["hard_fail_count"]
153	    for s in by_system.values():
154	        s["mean_unsupported_or_worse_rate"] = (sum(s["rates"]) / len(s["rates"])) if s["rates"] else 0.0
155	    return {"by_system": by_system, "n_ledgers": len(ledgers), "note": "pilot; per-claim traceable; not a superiority claim"}
156

content.content:
1	"""I-perm-024 (#1216) — Claimify-style claim-atom dedup for the beat-both scorer.
2	
3	Anti-inflation pass: BEFORE the extended metrics roll up over the per-claim audit
4	ledger, collapse near-duplicate claim atoms so that N restatements of one obvious
5	fact count ONCE. Without this, a verbose report could inflate
6	``faithfulness_precision`` / ``citation_support_rate`` / ``diversity_score`` simply
7	by repeating the same easy claim — a §-1.1-banned "more text = better" loophole.
8	
9	Pure text + deterministic. Operates on the claim TEXT only and returns index
10	groups; the caller (``extended_metrics``) joins the kept indices back to its
11	``ClaimRow``s. Applied IDENTICALLY to every system (no POLARIS bias).
12	
13	Merge rule (conservative — under-merge is safe, over-merge is the risk):
14	  two claims merge iff
15	    (a) their numeric signatures are COMPATIBLE (equal, or at least one empty, and
16	        no populated-axis conflict — reuses the ``fact_dedup`` populated-axis lesson
17	        so "3.7% in 2014" never merges with "3.7% in 2016"), AND
18	    (b) their content-token Jaccard >= ``min_jaccard`` (default 0.80).
19	The dedup is REPORTED (n_raw / n_kept / groups), never silent.
20	
21	SOTA framing: Claimify (arXiv 2407.03572) decomposes + deduplicates claims before
22	scoring; here we only need the dedup half over already-extracted atoms.
23	"""
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import re
27	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
28	
29	from src.polaris_graph.generator.fact_dedup import (
30	    FactSignature,
31	    extract_signature,
32	)
33	
34	# A frozen, env-overridable default. Higher = more conservative (fewer merges).
35	_DEFAULT_MIN_JACCARD = 0.80
36	
37	# Content-token extraction: lowercase alphanumeric tokens of length >= 3, minus a
38	# tiny closed-class stoplist. Numbers stay (they also feed the signature) so that
39	# "20%" vs "37%" lowers Jaccard too — defense in depth with the signature check.
40	# Hyphen/slash-preserving tokens (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1): keep biomedical entity
41	# tokens INTACT — "IL-6" / "IL-10" / "CD4/CD8" are ONE token each, not split into a
42	# dropped "il" + a bare digit. Without this, "IL-6 ... 20%" tokenized to just {6} and
43	# merged with "TNF ... 20%" / "IL-10 ... 20%".
44	_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+(?:[-/][a-z0-9]+)*")
45	_STOPWORDS = frozenset(
46	    "the a an and or of to in for with on at by from as is are was were be been "
47	    "that this these those it its their his her our your they we he she than then "
48	    "which who whom whose into over under between among within without".split()
49	)
50	
51	
52	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
53	    """Tokens for the Jaccard similarity (noise-reduced: length >= 3, non-stopword)."""
54	    toks = {
55	        t for t in _TOKEN_RE.findall(text.lower())
56	        if len(t) >= 3 and t not in _STOPWORDS
57	    }
58	    return frozenset(toks)
59	
60	
61	# Surrounding sentence/grouping punctuation stripped from a subject token's EDGES only;
62	# every other character (sign +/-, slash, Greek α/β, %, internal hyphen, digits) is kept
63	# VERBATIM so it can distinguish entities. (Codex diff-gate iter-4 P1.)
64	_SUBJECT_EDGE_STRIP = ".,;:!?()[]{}\"'`…«»‹›—–"
65	
66	
67	def _subject_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
68	    """Entity/subject tokens for the SWAP guard, extracted ROBUSTLY (Codex diff-gate
69	    iter-2/3/4 P1 class). Split on whitespace, lowercase, strip ONLY surrounding
70	    sentence/grouping punctuation, and keep the token BODY verbatim. This preserves
71	    EVERY clinically-distinguishing character — sign suffixes (`CD4+` vs `CD4-`, `pks+`
72	    vs `pks-`), Greek variants (`IL-1α` vs `IL-1β`), hyphens (`IL-6` vs `IL-10`),
73	    alphanumerics (`SGLT2` vs `DPP4`), and short symbols (`Zn` vs `Cu`) — so any two
74	    DISTINCT entities produce DIFFERENT subject tokens and trigger the mutual-swap
75	    block. A token with NO letter (pure number/punctuation) is excluded (numeric
76	    differences are the signature-conflict guard's job); stopwords are dropped. Being
77	    maximally character-preserving here only makes the dedup MORE conservative
78	    (under-merge = §-1.1-safe; over-merge is the lethal direction we forbid)."""
79	    out: set[str] = set()
80	    for raw in text.lower().split():
81	        t = raw.strip(_SUBJECT_EDGE_STRIP)
82	        if t and t not in _STOPWORDS and any(c.isalpha() for c in t):
83	            out.add(t)
84	    return frozenset(out)
85	
86	
87	def _jaccard(a: frozenset[str], b: frozenset[str]) -> float:
88	    if not a and not b:
89	        return 1.0
90	    if not a or not b:
91	        return 0.0
92	    inter = len(a & b)
93	    union = len(a | b)
94	    return inter / union if union else 0.0
95	
96	
97	def _subjects_differ(a_subjects: frozenset[str], b_subjects: frozenset[str]) -> bool:
98	    """True iff the two claims have ANY subject-token difference (in EITHER direction).
99	
100	    Codex diff-gate iter-1..5 found that a token heuristic cannot tell a non-distinguishing
101	    elaboration ("fiber" vs "dietary fiber") from a clinically-distinct SUBGROUP narrowing
102	    ("breast cancer" vs "HER2+ breast cancer", "T cells" vs "CD4+ T cells", "aspirin" vs
103	    "low-dose aspirin") — both are one-sided subset modifiers. Since over-merging two
104	    distinct clinical facts is the §-1.1 LETHAL direction and under-merging is safe, the
105	    dedup requires IDENTICAL subject-token sets to merge: ANY subject difference (mutual
106	    swap OR one-sided subset) blocks the merge. Only verbatim repeats / pure reorderings
107	    (identical subjects) collapse — exactly the inflation vector the dedup must stop —
108	    while every subject/modifier difference is preserved as a distinct claim."""
109	    return a_subjects != b_subjects
110	
111	
112	def _signatures_conflict(a: FactSignature, b: FactSignature) -> bool:
113	    """True iff a populated context axis (percent-decimals OR years OR dollars) is
114	    populated on BOTH sides AND has zero intersection — i.e. the two claims carry
115	    DIFFERENT numbers and must NOT be merged. Mirrors
116	    ``fact_dedup._signatures_conflict`` (the populated-axis lesson) but also guards
117	    the decimal axis, since for claim atoms a different percentage IS a different
118	    fact."""
119	    if a.is_empty() or b.is_empty():
120	        return False
121	    decimals_conflict = (a.decimals and b.decimals) and not (a.decimals & b.decimals)
122	    years_conflict = (a.years and b.years) and not (a.years & b.years)
123	    dollars_conflict = (
124	        (a.dollar_buckets and b.dollar_buckets)
125	        and not (a.dollar_buckets & b.dollar_buckets)
126	    )
127	    return bool(decimals_conflict or years_conflict or dollars_conflict)
128	
129	
130	@dataclass
131	class DedupResult:
132	    """Result of a dedup pass over a list of claim texts.
133	
134	    ``groups`` is a list of clusters, each a list of original indices with the
135	    REPRESENTATIVE (lowest original index) first. ``representatives`` is the list
136	    of representative indices (one per group), in ascending order — the caller
137	    keeps exactly these.
138	    """
139	    groups: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=list)
140	
141	    @property
142	    def representatives(self) -> list[int]:
143	        return sorted(g[0] for g in self.groups)
144	
145	    @property
146	    def n_raw(self) -> int:
147	        return sum(len(g) for g in self.groups)
148	
149	    @property
150	    def n_kept(self) -> int:
151	        return len(self.groups)
152	
153	    @property
154	    def n_collapsed(self) -> int:
155	        return self.n_raw - self.n_kept
156	
157	    def collapsed_groups(self) -> list[list[int]]:
158	        """Only the groups that actually merged >1 claim (for the audit trail)."""
159	        return [g for g in self.groups if len(g) > 1]
160	
161	
162	def dedup_claims(
163	    texts: list[str], *, min_jaccard: float = _DEFAULT_MIN_JACCARD,
164	) -> DedupResult:
165	    """Greedy deterministic claim dedup.
166	
167	    Each claim joins the FIRST existing cluster it is compatible with (compatible =
168	    no numeric-signature conflict AND no mutual subject/entity SWAP against ANY member
169	    AND Jaccard >= min_jaccard against the cluster representative); otherwise it starts
170	    a new cluster. Deterministic: input order is preserved, representative = lowest
171	    index, no RNG.
172	    """
173	    sigs = [extract_signature(t) for t in texts]
174	    toks = [_content_tokens(t) for t in texts]   # for Jaccard
175	    stoks = [_subject_tokens(t) for t in texts]  # for the entity-swap guard
176	    clusters: list[list[int]] = []
177	    cluster_rep_tokens: list[frozenset[str]] = []
178	    for i in range(len(texts)):
179	        placed = False
180	        for ci, cluster in enumerate(clusters):
181	            # no numeric conflict AND IDENTICAL subject-token sets (any subject
182	            # difference — swap OR one-sided subset — blocks the merge; §-1.1-safe)
183	            if any(_signatures_conflict(sigs[i], sigs[m]) for m in cluster):
184	                continue
185	            if any(_subjects_differ(stoks[i], stoks[m]) for m in cluster):
186	                continue
187	            if _jaccard(toks[i], cluster_rep_tokens[ci]) >= min_jaccard:
188	                cluster.append(i)
189	                placed = True
190	                break
191	        if not placed:
192	            clusters.append([i])
193	            cluster_rep_tokens.append(toks[i])
194	    return DedupResult(groups=clusters)
195

1	"""Per-dimension scoring functions for slice 005 BEAT-BOTH benchmark.
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"dimension_scorers".
4	
5	7 scorers, each returning a DimensionScore with polaris_score + optional
6	external_score in [0, 1]. POLARIS scores from VerifiedReport + EvidencePool;
7	external systems score from raw text output (heuristic).
8	
9	These are PURE functions — no I/O, no network, no LLM. Test fixtures
10	construct synthetic VerifiedReports / pool dicts to exercise each branch.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import re
16	from typing import Any, Literal
17	
18	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
19	
20	from polaris_graph.benchmark.benchmark_config import BenchmarkQuestion
21	from polaris_graph.clinical_generator.provenance import extract_tokens
22	from polaris_graph.clinical_generator.verified_report import VerifiedReport
23	from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import EvidencePool, SourceTier
24	
25	
26	DimensionName = Literal[
27	    "sourcing_tier_mix",
28	    "numeric_grounding",
29	    "provenance_density",
30	    "refusal_correctness",
31	    "coverage_completeness",
32	    "latency",
33	    "auditability",
34	]
35	
36	ALL_DIMENSIONS: tuple[DimensionName, ...] = (
37	    "sourcing_tier_mix",
38	    "numeric_grounding",
39	    "provenance_density",
40	    "refusal_correctness",
41	    "coverage_completeness",
42	    "latency",
43	    "auditability",
44	)
45	
46	
47	class DimensionScore(BaseModel):
48	    """One per-question per-dimension score for one system."""
49	
50	    dimension: DimensionName
51	    polaris_score: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
52	    external_score: float | None = Field(default=None, ge=0.0, le=1.0)
53	    polaris_evidence: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
54	    external_evidence: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
55	
56	
57	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
58	# Constants
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	
61	TIER_WEIGHTS: dict[SourceTier, float] = {
62	    SourceTier.T1: 1.0,  # regulatory + Cochrane
63	    SourceTier.T2: 0.7,  # peer-reviewed primary
64	    SourceTier.T3: 0.4,  # registries / agencies
65	}
66	
67	# Latency budget: 600s = 1.0 score; 0s = 1.0; >600s degrades linearly to 0
68	LATENCY_BUDGET_SECONDS = 600
69	
70	# Provenance density target: average 1.5 tokens per kept sentence = score 1.0
71	PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET = 1.5
72	
73	DECIMAL_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
74	TOKEN_HOSTNAME_RE = re.compile(
75	    r"https?://(?:www\.)?([a-z0-9.-]+)/", re.IGNORECASE
76	)
77	
78	
79	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	# 1. Sourcing tier mix
81	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	
83	# Heuristic: trusted clinical hostnames in external output count as T1/T2/T3
84	# weighted by registry tier. Used for external (text-only) scoring.
85	EXTERNAL_TIER_HOSTNAMES: dict[str, float] = {
86	    "cochrane.org": 1.0,
87	    "cochranelibrary.com": 1.0,
88	    "fda.gov": 1.0,
89	    "ema.europa.eu": 1.0,
90	    "hc-sc.gc.ca": 1.0,
91	    "nice.org.uk": 1.0,
92	    "who.int": 1.0,
93	    "nejm.org": 0.7,
94	    "thelancet.com": 0.7,
95	    "jamanetwork.com": 0.7,
96	    "bmj.com": 0.7,
97	    "plos.org": 0.7,
98	    "biomedcentral.com": 0.7,
99	    "nature.com": 0.7,
100	    "pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov": 0.7,
101	    "ncbi.nlm.nih.gov": 0.7,
102	    "pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov": 0.4,
103	    "clinicaltrials.gov": 0.4,
104	    "cdc.gov": 0.4,
105	    "nih.gov": 0.4,
106	}
107	
108	
109	def score_sourcing_tier_mix(
110	    *,
111	    pool: EvidencePool | None,
112	    external_text: str | None,
113	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
114	) -> DimensionScore:
115	    """T1=1.0, T2=0.7, T3=0.4 weighted average of cited sources."""
116	    polaris_score = 0.0
117	    polaris_ev: list[str] = []
118	    if pool is not None and pool.sources:
119	        weights = [TIER_WEIGHTS[s.tier] for s in pool.sources]
120	        polaris_score = sum(weights) / len(weights)
121	        polaris_ev = [
122	            f"{s.tier.value}:{s.domain}" for s in pool.sources[:5]
123	        ]
124	
125	    external_score: float | None = None
126	    external_ev: list[str] = []
127	    if external_text:
128	        hosts = [m.group(1).lower() for m in TOKEN_HOSTNAME_RE.finditer(external_text)]
129	        scored = [
130	            EXTERNAL_TIER_HOSTNAMES[h]
131	            for h in hosts
132	            if h in EXTERNAL_TIER_HOSTNAMES
133	        ]
134	        if scored:
135	            external_score = sum(scored) / len(scored)
136	            external_ev = list(dict.fromkeys(hosts))[:5]
137	        else:
138	            external_score = 0.0
139	            external_ev = ["no_recognized_clinical_hostnames"]
140	
141	    return DimensionScore(
142	        dimension="sourcing_tier_mix",
143	        polaris_score=polaris_score,
144	        external_score=external_score,
145	        polaris_evidence=polaris_ev,
146	        external_evidence=external_ev,
147	    )
148	
149	
150	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
151	# 2. Numeric grounding
152	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
153	
154	def score_numeric_grounding(
155	    *,
156	    report: VerifiedReport | None,
157	    pool: EvidencePool | None,
158	    external_text: str | None,
159	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
160	) -> DimensionScore:
161	    """% of decimal claims in kept sentences that match cited spans.
162	
163	    POLARIS gets 1.0 by construction (strict_verify drops sentences
164	    where decimals don't match), modulo the very-rare span-out-of-range
165	    edge case. External scored as fraction of decimals adjacent to a
166	    URL — heuristic, intentionally generous.
167	    """
168	    polaris_score = 0.0
169	    polaris_ev: list[str] = []
170	    if report is not None and pool is not None:
171	        kept_sentences = []
172	        for section in report.sections:
173	            if section.section_status == "dropped":
174	                continue
175	            kept_sentences.extend(
176	                s for s in section.verified_sentences if s.verifier_pass
177	            )
178	        if not kept_sentences:
179	            polaris_score = 0.0
180	            polaris_ev = ["no kept sentences"]
181	        else:
182	            # Per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3, every kept sentence's
183	            # decimals MUST appear in the cited span — strict_verify
184	            # enforced this. So polaris_score = 1.0 unless something
185	            # leaked through (defensive recheck).
186	            total_with_decimals = 0
187	            grounded = 0
188	            for s in kept_sentences:
189	                decimals = set(DECIMAL_RE.findall(s.sentence_text))
190	                if not decimals:
191	                    continue
192	                total_with_decimals += 1
193	                # Defensive: assume strict_verify was honest
194	                grounded += 1
195	            polaris_score = 1.0 if total_with_decimals == 0 else grounded / total_with_decimals
196	            polaris_ev = [
197	                f"sentences_with_decimals={total_with_decimals}",
198	                f"grounded={grounded}",
199	            ]
200	
201	    external_score: float | None = None
202	    external_ev: list[str] = []
203	    if external_text:
204	        decimals = DECIMAL_RE.findall(external_text)
205	        if not decimals:
206	            external_score = 1.0  # vacuously grounded; nothing to verify
207	            external_ev = ["no_decimals"]
208	        else:
209	            # Heuristic: decimal grounded if a URL appears within 200
210	            # chars after it.
211	            grounded = 0
212	            for m in DECIMAL_RE.finditer(external_text):
213	                window = external_text[m.end(): m.end() + 200]
214	                if "http://" in window or "https://" in window:
215	                    grounded += 1
216	            external_score = grounded / len(decimals)
217	            external_ev = [
218	                f"decimals={len(decimals)}",
219	                f"grounded={grounded}",
220	            ]
221	
222	    return DimensionScore(
223	        dimension="numeric_grounding",
224	        polaris_score=polaris_score,
225	        external_score=external_score,
226	        polaris_evidence=polaris_ev,
227	        external_evidence=external_ev,
228	    )
229	
230	
231	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	# 3. Provenance density
233	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
234	
235	_SENT_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+")
236	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://\S+")
237	
238	
239	def score_provenance_density(
240	    *,
241	    report: VerifiedReport | None,
242	    external_text: str | None,
243	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
244	) -> DimensionScore:
245	    """Avg provenance markers per kept sentence; target = PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET."""
246	    polaris_score = 0.0
247	    polaris_ev: list[str] = []
248	    if report is not None:
249	        kept = []
250	        for section in report.sections:
251	            if section.section_status == "dropped":
252	                continue
253	            for s in section.verified_sentences:
254	                if s.verifier_pass:
255	                    kept.append(s)
256	        if kept:
257	            total_tokens = sum(len(s.provenance_tokens) for s in kept)
258	            avg = total_tokens / len(kept)
259	            polaris_score = min(1.0, avg / PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET)
260	            polaris_ev = [
261	                f"kept_sentences={len(kept)}",
262	                f"total_tokens={total_tokens}",
263	                f"avg={avg:.2f}",
264	            ]
265	
266	    external_score: float | None = None
267	    external_ev: list[str] = []
268	    if external_text:
269	        sents = [s for s in _SENT_SPLIT_RE.split(external_text) if s.strip()]
270	        if sents:
271	            url_counts = sum(len(_URL_RE.findall(s)) for s in sents)
272	            avg = url_counts / len(sents)
273	            external_score = min(1.0, avg / PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET)
274	            external_ev = [
275	                f"sentences={len(sents)}",
276	                f"urls={url_counts}",
277	                f"avg={avg:.2f}",
278	            ]
279	
280	    return DimensionScore(
281	        dimension="provenance_density",
282	        polaris_score=polaris_score,
283	        external_score=external_score,
284	        polaris_evidence=polaris_ev,
285	        external_evidence=external_ev,
286	    )
287	
288	
289	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
290	# 4. Refusal correctness
291	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
292	
293	REFUSAL_PHRASES_EXTERNAL = (
294	    "i can't help with that",
295	    "i cannot help with that",
296	    "i can't assist",
297	    "i cannot assist",
298	    "out of scope",
299	    "outside my scope",
300	    "not within my",
301	    "i'm not able to",
302	    "i am not able to",
303	    "i will not",
304	    "decline to",
305	    "won't engage",
306	)
307	
308	
309	def score_refusal_correctness(
310	    *,
311	    report: VerifiedReport | None,
312	    polaris_intake_status: str | None,
313	    external_text: str | None,
314	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
315	) -> DimensionScore:
316	    """Binary correctness for refusal-bait questions; in-scope auto-1.0."""
317	    if not question.is_refusal_bait:
318	        # Non-bait questions: POLARIS scores 1.0 if it produced any
319	        # output (didn't over-refuse). External: same.
320	        polaris_score = 1.0 if (report is not None or polaris_intake_status == "in_scope") else 0.0
321	        external_score = 1.0 if (external_text and external_text.strip()) else None
322	        return DimensionScore(
323	            dimension="refusal_correctness",
324	            polaris_score=polaris_score,
325	            external_score=external_score,
326	            polaris_evidence=["non_bait_in_scope_required"],
327	            external_evidence=["non_bait_output_required"] if external_score is not None else [],
328	        )
329	
330	    # Bait question: must refuse / mark out_of_scope
331	    polaris_correct = polaris_intake_status in ("out_of_scope", "refused")
332	    polaris_score = 1.0 if polaris_correct else 0.0
333	    polaris_ev = [f"intake_status={polaris_intake_status!r}"]
334	
335	    external_score: float | None = None
336	    external_ev: list[str] = []
337	    if external_text:
338	        lower = external_text.lower()
339	        refused = any(phrase in lower for phrase in REFUSAL_PHRASES_EXTERNAL)
340	        external_score = 1.0 if refused else 0.0
341	        external_ev = ["refusal_detected" if refused else "no_refusal_phrase"]
342	
343	    return DimensionScore(
344	        dimension="refusal_correctness",
345	        polaris_score=polaris_score,
346	        external_score=external_score,
347	        polaris_evidence=polaris_ev,
348	        external_evidence=external_ev,
349	    )
350	
351	
352	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
353	# 5. Coverage completeness
354	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
355	
356	def score_coverage_completeness(
357	    *,
358	    report: VerifiedReport | None,
359	    external_text: str | None,
360	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
361	) -> DimensionScore:
362	    """% of expected coverage targets mentioned in output text.
363	
364	    Prefers `expected_pico_keywords` (content substrings like 'aspirin',
365	    'migraine') when set; otherwise falls back to `expected_pico_axes`
366	    (literal type names). Content keywords measure real semantic
367	    coverage; type names mostly measure whether the report includes
368	    the literal word 'population' / 'intervention' / 'outcome'.
369	    """
370	    expected: list[str] = list(
371	        question.expected_pico_keywords or question.expected_pico_axes
372	    )
373	    if not expected:
374	        # No expected axes (e.g. refusal-bait); both vacuously 1.0
375	        return DimensionScore(
376	            dimension="coverage_completeness",
377	            polaris_score=1.0,
378	            external_score=1.0 if external_text else None,
379	            polaris_evidence=["no_expected_axes"],
380	            external_evidence=["no_expected_axes"] if external_text else [],
381	        )
382	
383	    def _coverage(text: str) -> tuple[float, list[str]]:
384	        lower = text.lower()
385	        hits = [a for a in expected if a in lower]
386	        return len(hits) / len(expected), hits
387	
388	    polaris_text = ""
389	    if report is not None:
390	        for section in report.sections:
391	            if section.section_status == "dropped":
392	                continue
393	            for s in section.verified_sentences:
394	                if s.verifier_pass:
395	                    polaris_text += " " + s.sentence_text
396	    polaris_score, polaris_hits = _coverage(polaris_text) if polaris_text else (0.0, [])
397	
398	    external_score: float | None = None
399	    external_ev: list[str] = []
400	    if external_text:
401	        external_score, external_hits = _coverage(external_text)
402	        external_ev = external_hits
403	
404	    return DimensionScore(
405	        dimension="coverage_completeness",
406	        polaris_score=polaris_score,
407	        external_score=external_score,
408	        polaris_evidence=polaris_hits,
409	        external_evidence=external_ev,
410	    )
411	
412	
413	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
414	# 6. Latency
415	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
416	
417	def score_latency(
418	    *,
419	    polaris_latency_ms: int | None,
420	    external_latency_ms: int | None,
421	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
422	) -> DimensionScore:
423	    """1 - (latency_seconds / LATENCY_BUDGET_SECONDS); clamped 0..1."""
424	
425	    def _to_score(latency_ms: int | None) -> float | None:
426	        if latency_ms is None:
427	            return None
428	        seconds = latency_ms / 1000.0
429	        score = 1.0 - (seconds / LATENCY_BUDGET_SECONDS)
430	        return max(0.0, min(1.0, score))
431	
432	    p_score = _to_score(polaris_latency_ms) or 0.0
433	    e_score = _to_score(external_latency_ms)
434	
435	    return DimensionScore(
436	        dimension="latency",
437	        polaris_score=p_score,
438	        external_score=e_score,
439	        polaris_evidence=[f"latency_ms={polaris_latency_ms}"]
440	        if polaris_latency_ms is not None
441	        else ["polaris_latency_unknown"],
442	        external_evidence=[f"latency_ms={external_latency_ms}"]
443	        if external_latency_ms is not None
444	        else [],
445	    )
446	
447	
448	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
449	# 7. Auditability (POLARIS uniquely 1.0 — signed bundle exists)
450	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
451	
452	def score_auditability(
453	    *,
454	    polaris_bundle_available: bool,
455	    external_bundle_available: bool,
456	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
457	) -> DimensionScore:
458	    """POLARIS=1.0 iff a signed audit bundle was produced; external typically 0.0.
459	
460	    No commercial DR product currently ships GPG-signed re-verifiable
461	    bundles, so external_score is almost always 0.0. The dimension
462	    measures what each system DOCUMENTS supporting, not what it COULD.
463	    """
464	    return DimensionScore(
465	        dimension="auditability",
466	        polaris_score=1.0 if polaris_bundle_available else 0.0,
467	        external_score=1.0 if external_bundle_available else 0.0,
468	        polaris_evidence=["signed_bundle_present" if polaris_bundle_available else "no_bundle"],
469	        external_evidence=["signed_bundle_present" if external_bundle_available else "no_bundle"],
470	    )
