1	"""I-perm-024 (#1216) — beat-both scorer metric extension (claim-by-claim only).
2	
3	Five extended metrics for the DR head-to-head, computed STRICTLY from the
4	already-audited per-claim ledger (``ClaimRow``s — each a reconciled VERIFIED/
5	PARTIAL/UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE verdict against the FETCHED cited span)
6	and the FROZEN gold rubric (``RubricElement``s whose ``covered`` /
7	``citation_supported`` flags were set by the SAME dual §-1.1 audit).
8	
9	§-1.1 STRUCTURAL GUARANTEE (the load-bearing safety property): every function here
10	accepts ONLY ``ClaimRow`` / ``RubricElement`` typed inputs. None of them receives or
11	opens raw report text. So they CANNOT do string-presence / pattern-presence matching
12	("does the report mention 'tirzepatide'?") — that is structurally impossible, not a
13	convention. Each metric is a roll-up over the per-claim audit LEDGER, never over raw
14	text. This is what keeps ``required_entity_recall`` / ``diversity_score`` off the
15	§-1.1 BANNED list (word/citation/source COUNTS as quality, pattern-presence,
16	string-presence PASS/FAIL — "lethal in clinical").
17	
18	The metrics:
19	  1. ``faithfulness_precision`` = VERIFIED / material(S0-S2).
20	  2. ``citation_support_rate`` = VERIFIED-with-a-RESOLVED-citation / material.
21	  3. ``required_entity_recall`` = covered+citation_supported frozen rubric elements
22	     / total (the existing ``lane2_coverage`` semantics, surfaced as recall).
23	  4. ``safety_floor_recall`` = #3 restricted to the PRE-REGISTERED safety element
24	     subset (``safety_floor_elements_v3.json``, pinned to the rubric sha).
25	  5. ``diversity_score`` = distinct resolved sources among VERIFIED claims / VERIFIED
26	     count — DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (support-concentration), NOT a superiority signal.
27	
28	Claimify-style dedup (``claim_dedup``) collapses near-duplicate claim atoms BEFORE
29	#1/#2/#5 so a verbose report cannot inflate by restating one easy fact. Applied
30	identically to every system.
31	"""
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	import json
35	from dataclasses import dataclass
36	from pathlib import Path
37	
38	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer import ClaimRow, RubricElement
39	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_dedup import dedup_claims
40	
41	_MATERIAL_SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2")
42	
43	# Default location of the pre-registered safety-floor element registry. Lives under
44	# tracked config/ (NOT runtime outputs/, which is gitignored) — it is hand-authored
45	# pre-registration, committed, not a regenerated artifact.
46	_SAFETY_FLOOR_REGISTRY = (
47	    Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
48	    / "config" / "dr_benchmark" / "safety_floor_elements_v3.json"
49	)
50	
51	_DIVERSITY_NOTE = (
52	    "diversity_score is a support-concentration DIAGNOSTIC (distinct sources among "
53	    "VERIFIED claims / VERIFIED count), NOT a superiority signal; a higher value is "
54	    "NOT a §-1.1 'win' (unique-source counts are banned as quality signals)."
55	)
56	
57	
58	@dataclass(frozen=True)
59	class ScoredClaim:
60	    """One audited claim paired with its prose text (text is used ONLY for dedup —
61	    never for verdict assignment, which already happened upstream)."""
62	    text: str
63	    row: ClaimRow
64	
65	
66	def _is_material(row: ClaimRow) -> bool:
67	    return row.severity in _MATERIAL_SEVERITIES
68	
69	
70	def _has_resolved_citation(row: ClaimRow) -> bool:
71	    """A claim is citation-supported only if it carries a non-empty resolved
72	    citation id (an unresolved / None citation does NOT count — traceability floor)."""
73	    return bool(row.citation_id)
74	
75	
76	def faithfulness_precision(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
77	    material = [r for r in rows if _is_material(r)]
78	    denom = len(material)
79	    verified = sum(1 for r in material if r.verdict == "VERIFIED")
80	    return {
81	        "material_atoms": denom,
82	        "verified": verified,
83	        "value": (verified / denom) if denom else None,
84	    }
85	
86	
87	def citation_support_rate(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
88	    material = [r for r in rows if _is_material(r)]
89	    denom = len(material)
90	    supported = sum(
91	        1 for r in material if r.verdict == "VERIFIED" and _has_resolved_citation(r)
92	    )
93	    return {
94	        "material_atoms": denom,
95	        "verified_and_cited": supported,
96	        "value": (supported / denom) if denom else None,
97	    }
98	
99	
100	def diversity_score(rows: list[ClaimRow]) -> dict:
101	    verified = [r for r in rows if r.verdict == "VERIFIED"]
102	    denom = len(verified)
103	    distinct_sources = {r.citation_id for r in verified if r.citation_id}
104	    return {
105	        "verified_claims": denom,
106	        "distinct_sources": len(distinct_sources),
107	        "value": (len(distinct_sources) / denom) if denom else None,
108	        "note": _DIVERSITY_NOTE,
109	    }
110	
111	
112	def required_entity_recall(rubric: list[RubricElement] | None) -> dict:
113	    if not rubric:
114	        return {"total_required": 0, "covered_supported": 0, "value": None,
115	                "pending": True}
116	    total = len(rubric)
117	    covered_supported = sum(1 for e in rubric if e.covered and e.citation_supported)
118	    return {
119	        "total_required": total,
120	        "covered_supported": covered_supported,
121	        "value": (covered_supported / total) if total else None,
122	        "missing": [e.element_id for e in rubric
123	                    if not (e.covered and e.citation_supported)],
124	        "pending": False,
125	    }
126	
127	
128	def safety_floor_recall(
129	    rubric: list[RubricElement] | None, safety_element_ids: set[str] | None,
130	) -> dict:
131	    if not rubric or not safety_element_ids:
132	        return {"total_safety_required": 0, "covered_supported": 0, "value": None,
133	                "pending": True}
134	    # Denominator = the PRE-REGISTERED tagged count, NOT the count present in the
135	    # supplied rubric (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2): a tagged id missing from the rubric
136	    # must SURFACE + count AGAINST recall (fail-safe), never silently shrink the
137	    # denominator into a falsely-high recall.
138	    rubric_by_id = {e.element_id: e for e in rubric}
139	    total = len(safety_element_ids)
140	    covered_supported = 0
141	    missing: list[str] = []
142	    missing_from_rubric: list[str] = []
143	    for eid in sorted(safety_element_ids):
144	        e = rubric_by_id.get(eid)
145	        if e is None:
146	            missing_from_rubric.append(eid)
147	            missing.append(eid)
148	            continue
149	        if e.covered and e.citation_supported:
150	            covered_supported += 1
151	        else:
152	            missing.append(eid)
153	    return {
154	        "total_safety_required": total,
155	        "covered_supported": covered_supported,
156	        "value": (covered_supported / total) if total else None,
157	        "missing": missing,
158	        "missing_from_rubric": missing_from_rubric,
159	        "pending": False,
160	    }
161	
162	
163	def load_safety_floor_element_ids(
164	    question_id: str, *, registry_path: Path | None = None,
165	) -> set[str]:
166	    """Read the pre-registered safety-floor element ids for one question from
167	    ``safety_floor_elements_v3.json`` (transparent tagging of the frozen rubric;
168	    NOT invented claims). Missing file / question → empty set (safety_floor_recall
169	    then reports pending, never a false 0)."""
170	    path = registry_path or _SAFETY_FLOOR_REGISTRY
171	    if not path.exists():
172	        return set()
173	    try:
174	        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
175	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
176	        return set()
177	    qmap = data.get("questions", {})
178	    entry = qmap.get(str(question_id)) or qmap.get(f"Q{question_id}")
179	    if not entry:
180	        return set()
181	    return {str(eid) for eid in entry.get("safety_floor_element_ids", [])}
182	
183	
184	def _verdict_aware_keep(
185	    scored_claims: list[ScoredClaim], groups: list[list[int]],
186	) -> tuple[list[ClaimRow], int]:
187	    """Collapse ONLY repeated VERIFIED claims; keep EVERY non-VERIFIED claim
188	    (Codex brief-gate iter-1 P2: a dedup must never hide a bad verdict — if a
189	    cluster mixes a VERIFIED restatement with an UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/PARTIAL/
190	    UNREACHABLE near-duplicate, the bad verdict survives uncollapsed).
191	
192	    Within each text cluster: keep the FIRST VERIFIED row (collapsing the rest of
193	    the VERIFIED restatements to one verified unit) PLUS every non-VERIFIED row
194	    untouched. Returns (kept_rows, n_verified_collapsed)."""
195	    kept: list[ClaimRow] = []
196	    n_verified_collapsed = 0
197	    for cluster in groups:
198	        rows_in = [scored_claims[i].row for i in cluster]
199	        verified = [r for r in rows_in if r.verdict == "VERIFIED"]
200	        non_verified = [r for r in rows_in if r.verdict != "VERIFIED"]
201	        if verified:
202	            # Deterministic, disclosed representative choice (Codex brief-gate iter-2
203	            # P2): when VERIFIED duplicates have mixed citation resolution, keep the
204	            # FIRST one carrying a RESOLVED citation so citation_support_rate reflects
205	            # the best-supported instance; else keep the first VERIFIED. Stable by
206	            # original cluster order.
207	            rep = next((r for r in verified if r.citation_id), verified[0])
208	            kept.append(rep)
209	            n_verified_collapsed += len(verified) - 1
210	        kept.extend(non_verified)
211	    return kept, n_verified_collapsed
212	
213	
214	def compute_extended_metrics(
215	    scored_claims: list[ScoredClaim],
216	    *,
217	    rubric: list[RubricElement] | None = None,
218	    safety_element_ids: set[str] | None = None,
219	    min_jaccard: float = 0.80,
220	) -> dict:
221	    """The 5 metrics for ONE (system × question). Dedups REPEATED VERIFIED claim
222	    atoms first (anti-inflation, never hiding a bad verdict), then rolls up over the
223	    kept ClaimRows + the audited frozen rubric. Pure aggregation — assigns no
224	    verdicts, touches no gate.
225	
226	    ``scored_claims`` is a list of ``ScoredClaim(text, row)`` — a typed AUDITED
227	    carrier (the claim's prose text paired with its already-reconciled ``ClaimRow``).
228	    The text is used ONLY for the Claimify dedup; every metric value is derived from
229	    the ``ClaimRow`` verdicts + the rubric, never from the text (the §-1.1 guard)."""
230	    texts = [sc.text for sc in scored_claims]
231	    dedup = dedup_claims(texts, min_jaccard=min_jaccard)
232	    kept_rows, n_verified_collapsed = _verdict_aware_keep(scored_claims, dedup.groups)
233	
234	    return {
235	        "n_raw_claims": len(scored_claims),
236	        "n_kept_claims": len(kept_rows),
237	        "dedup": {
238	            "n_text_clusters_collapsed": dedup.n_collapsed,
239	            "n_verified_collapsed": n_verified_collapsed,
240	            "collapsed_groups": dedup.collapsed_groups(),
241	            "min_jaccard": min_jaccard,
242	            "policy": "collapse repeated VERIFIED only; every non-VERIFIED claim kept "
243	                      "(a bad verdict is never hidden by a VERIFIED near-duplicate).",
244	        },
245	        "faithfulness_precision": faithfulness_precision(kept_rows),
246	        "citation_support_rate": citation_support_rate(kept_rows),
247	        "diversity_score": diversity_score(kept_rows),
248	        "required_entity_recall": required_entity_recall(rubric),
249	        "safety_floor_recall": safety_floor_recall(rubric, safety_element_ids),
250	        "methodology_note": (
251	            "all metrics derived from the per-claim audit ledger (VERIFIED verdicts "
252	            "vs FETCHED cited spans) + the frozen gold rubric; NEVER from raw report "
253	            "text; dedup applied identically to every system; NOT a superiority claim."
254	        ),
255	    }
256

1	"""Load manually-exported ChatGPT/Gemini DR outputs for BEAT-BOTH scoring.
2	
3	Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"external_loader".
4	
5	Operators run benchmark questions against ChatGPT Deep Research and
6	Gemini DR via the respective web UIs and save outputs as plain .txt
7	files named `{question_id}.txt` in a per-system directory:
8	
9	    external_outputs/chatgpt/Q01_aspirin_migraine_efficacy.txt
10	    external_outputs/chatgpt/Q02_metformin_safety_older_adults.txt
11	    external_outputs/gemini/Q01_aspirin_migraine_efficacy.txt
12	    ...
13	
14	Missing files are non-fatal: the benchmark continues with N/A entries
15	in the scoreboard for those question×system pairs. Operators can
16	re-export later and re-run scoring.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import logging
22	from dataclasses import dataclass
23	from pathlib import Path
24	
25	_LOG = logging.getLogger(__name__)
26	
27	
28	# Per-output cap. Real ChatGPT/Gemini DR responses are typically 5-50KB
29	# of text; cap protects against pasted-debug-output / Word-doc
30	# accidents in the input directory.
31	MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES = 1 * 1024 * 1024  # 1 MB
32	
33	
34	@dataclass(frozen=True)
35	class ExternalLoadReport:
36	    """Result of loading a per-system output dir.
37	
38	    Used by report_renderer to display "X of N questions had outputs"
39	    so operators can see what's missing.
40	    """
41	
42	    system_name: str
43	    output_dir: Path
44	    expected_question_ids: tuple[str, ...]
45	    loaded: dict[str, str]                 # question_id -> text
46	    missing_question_ids: tuple[str, ...]
47	    extra_files: tuple[str, ...]           # files in dir not matching expected ids
48	    truncated_question_ids: tuple[str, ...]  # files exceeding MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES
49	
50	    def coverage_ratio(self) -> float:
51	        if not self.expected_question_ids:
52	            return 1.0
53	        return len(self.loaded) / len(self.expected_question_ids)
54	
55	
56	def load_external_outputs(
57	    system_name: str,
58	    output_dir: Path | None,
59	    expected_question_ids: list[str],
60	) -> ExternalLoadReport:
61	    """Load .txt files from `output_dir` matching `expected_question_ids`.
62	
63	    Args:
64	        system_name: e.g. 'chatgpt' or 'gemini' — used for diagnostics.
65	        output_dir: directory containing {question_id}.txt files. None
66	            means no outputs available; returns empty load report.
67	        expected_question_ids: list of question_id strings the benchmark
68	            cares about. Files with other names go into `extra_files`.
69	
70	    Returns:
71	        ExternalLoadReport with loaded dict + missing list + extras +
72	        truncated. Never raises on missing files; missing is recorded.
73	
74	    Raises:
75	        ValueError if `output_dir` exists but is not a directory.
76	    """
77	    if output_dir is None:
78	        return ExternalLoadReport(
79	            system_name=system_name,
80	            output_dir=Path("/dev/null"),
81	            expected_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
82	            loaded={},
83	            missing_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
84	            extra_files=(),
85	            truncated_question_ids=(),
86	        )
87	
88	    output_dir = Path(output_dir)
89	    if not output_dir.exists():
90	        _LOG.warning(
91	            "external output dir %s does not exist; treating as empty",
92	            output_dir,
93	        )
94	        return ExternalLoadReport(
95	            system_name=system_name,
96	            output_dir=output_dir,
97	            expected_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
98	            loaded={},
99	            missing_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
100	            extra_files=(),
101	            truncated_question_ids=(),
102	        )
103	    if not output_dir.is_dir():
104	        raise ValueError(
105	            f"external output path {output_dir} exists but is not a directory"
106	        )
107	
108	    expected_set = set(expected_question_ids)
109	    loaded: dict[str, str] = {}
110	    truncated: list[str] = []
111	    extras: list[str] = []
112	
113	    for child in sorted(output_dir.iterdir()):
114	        if not child.is_file():
115	            continue
116	        if child.suffix.lower() != ".txt":
117	            extras.append(child.name)
118	            continue
119	        qid = child.stem  # filename without .txt
120	        if qid not in expected_set:
121	            extras.append(child.name)
122	            continue
123	        try:
124	            data = child.read_bytes()
125	        except OSError as exc:
126	            _LOG.warning("failed to read %s: %s", child, exc)
127	            continue
128	        if len(data) > MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES:
129	            truncated.append(qid)
130	            data = data[:MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES]
131	        try:
132	            loaded[qid] = data.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
133	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
134	            # errors='replace' should never raise, but defensive
135	            loaded[qid] = data.decode("latin-1", errors="replace")
136	
137	    missing = sorted(expected_set - set(loaded.keys()))
138	
139	    return ExternalLoadReport(
140	        system_name=system_name,
141	        output_dir=output_dir,
142	        expected_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
143	        loaded=loaded,
144	        missing_question_ids=tuple(missing),
145	        extra_files=tuple(sorted(extras)),
146	        truncated_question_ids=tuple(sorted(truncated)),
147	    )
148

1	"""I-meta-006 (#1006) — FACT faithfulness scoring (evidence-locked judge harness).
2	
3	Turns extracted atoms into reconciled per-claim ``ClaimRow``s for
4	``claim_audit_scorer``, by running an EVIDENCE-LOCKED judge against the MOST
5	SPECIFIC cited span each system supplied (Codex design-gate APPROVE iter3).
6	
7	§-1.1 guarantees enforced HERE (not trusted from the judge):
8	  - The judge reads the FETCHED cited span, never title/abstract/metadata.
9	  - For VERIFIED / PARTIAL / FABRICATED, the judge's ``span_quote`` MUST be a
10	    literal substring of the fetched span; if not, the harness FAILS CLOSED to
11	    UNSUPPORTED (a judge that cannot produce a real supporting/refuting quote does
12	    NOT get to verify) — the quote cannot be fabricated (iter-1 P1-1).
13	  - Severity (S0-S3) is audit-assigned by the judge for EVERY atom, including
14	    uncited / unreachable ones (iter-2 P2-2); the harness never invents it.
15	  - MOST-SPECIFIC span per system, NO broader-source fallback; POLARIS ``[#ev:]``
16	    defines its span but confers NO auto-verification (iter-1 P1-4 / ruling q3).
17	  - Unresolved citation → UNREACHABLE(source_missing), kept in the denominator
18	    (iter-1 P2-2). Metadata/abstract-only → UNREACHABLE(metadata_only) (iter-2 P2-1).
19	
20	The ``span_fetcher`` and ``judge`` are INJECTED (the real fetcher + the
21	Claude+Codex reconciled-audit adapter run in the operator-gated paid run; tests
22	pass deterministic fakes — cash-free).
23	"""
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	from dataclasses import dataclass
27	from typing import Callable
28	
29	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer import ClaimRow
30	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.report_claim_extractor import (
31	    CitationRef,
32	    ExtractedAtom,
33	)
34	
35	_VALID_SEVERITIES = ("S0", "S1", "S2", "S3")
36	_QUOTE_REQUIRED_VERDICTS = ("VERIFIED", "PARTIAL", "FABRICATED")
37	
38	
39	@dataclass
40	class SpanResult:
41	    """What ``span_fetcher`` returns: the fetched cited span TEXT, or an
42	    unreachable subtype when the actual cited source text could not be fetched."""
43	    text: str | None
44	    unreachable_subtype: str | None = None   # set iff text is None
45	
46	
47	@dataclass
48	class JudgeVerdict:
49	    """What the injected evidence-locked judge returns for one atom."""
50	    verdict: str               # VERIFIED|PARTIAL|UNSUPPORTED|FABRICATED|UNREACHABLE
51	    severity: str              # S0|S1|S2|S3 (audit-assigned)
52	    span_quote: str | None = None     # exact supporting/refuting quote from the span
53	    audit_note: str | None = None
54	    unreachable_subtype: str | None = None
55	
56	
57	SpanFetcher = Callable[[CitationRef], SpanResult]
58	Judge = Callable[[str, "str | None", "CitationRef | None"], JudgeVerdict]
59	
60	
61	def _severity(jv: JudgeVerdict) -> str:
62	    return jv.severity if jv.severity in _VALID_SEVERITIES else "S2"
63	
64	
65	def _most_specific_ref(atom: ExtractedAtom) -> CitationRef | None:
66	    """The most specific cited anchor: a POLARIS [#ev:] span first, then a
67	    resolved author-year/numbered citation, then any cited ref. None = uncited."""
68	    cited = [c for c in atom.citation_refs if c.kind != "uncited"]
69	    if not cited:
70	        return None
71	    for c in cited:
72	        if c.kind == "ev_span":
73	            return c
74	    for c in cited:
75	        if c.resolved:
76	            return c
77	    return cited[0]
78	
79	
80	def _uncited_row(atom: ExtractedAtom, jv: JudgeVerdict) -> ClaimRow:
81	    # Uncited material claim cannot be verified — UNSUPPORTED, citation_id=null.
82	    return ClaimRow(
83	        claim_id=atom.atom_id, severity=_severity(jv), verdict="UNSUPPORTED",
84	        citation_id=None, span_quote=None,
85	        audit_note=jv.audit_note or "uncited material claim; no citation supplied",
86	    )
87	
88	
89	def _unreachable_row(
90	    atom: ExtractedAtom, ref: CitationRef, subtype: str, severity: str,
91	) -> ClaimRow:
92	    return ClaimRow(
93	        claim_id=atom.atom_id, severity=severity, verdict="UNREACHABLE",
94	        citation_id=(ref.resolved or ref.raw_key or None),
95	        span_quote=None, unreachable_subtype=subtype,
96	        audit_note=f"cited source not fetchable ({subtype}); verdict withheld",
97	    )
98	
99	
100	def _cited_row(
101	    atom: ExtractedAtom, ref: CitationRef, jv: JudgeVerdict, span_text: str,
102	) -> ClaimRow:
103	    verdict = jv.verdict
104	    span_quote = jv.span_quote
105	    audit_note = jv.audit_note
106	    citation_id = ref.resolved or ref.raw_key or None
107	
108	    # §-1.1 substring validation: a VERIFIED/PARTIAL/FABRICATED verdict MUST quote
109	    # a real span from the fetched text. If it does not, FAIL CLOSED to UNSUPPORTED
110	    # (the judge does not get to verify without a real quote).
111	    if verdict in _QUOTE_REQUIRED_VERDICTS:
112	        if not span_quote or span_quote not in span_text:
113	            return ClaimRow(
114	                claim_id=atom.atom_id, severity=_severity(jv), verdict="UNSUPPORTED",
115	                citation_id=citation_id, span_quote=None,
116	                audit_note="judge span_quote not found in fetched source; "
117	                           "verification withheld (fail-closed)",
118	            )
119	
120	    # A judge that returns UNREACHABLE when the span WAS fetched is contradictory
121	    # → treat as UNSUPPORTED (the source was available; absence of support is
122	    # unsupported, not unreachable).
123	    if verdict == "UNREACHABLE":
124	        return ClaimRow(
125	            claim_id=atom.atom_id, severity=_severity(jv), verdict="UNSUPPORTED",
126	            citation_id=citation_id, span_quote=None,
127	            audit_note="judge returned UNREACHABLE but cited span was fetched; "
128	                       "coerced to UNSUPPORTED",
129	        )
130	
131	    if verdict == "UNSUPPORTED":
132	        return ClaimRow(
133	            claim_id=atom.atom_id, severity=_severity(jv), verdict="UNSUPPORTED",
134	            citation_id=citation_id, span_quote=None,
135	            audit_note=audit_note or "no supporting span found in cited source",
136	        )
137	
138	    # VERIFIED / PARTIAL / FABRICATED with a validated real span_quote.
139	    return ClaimRow(
140	        claim_id=atom.atom_id, severity=_severity(jv), verdict=verdict,
141	        citation_id=citation_id, span_quote=span_quote, audit_note=audit_note,
142	    )
143	
144	
145	def score_atoms(
146	    atoms: list[ExtractedAtom],
147	    *,
148	    span_fetcher: SpanFetcher,
149	    judge: Judge,
150	) -> list[ClaimRow]:
151	    """Score every atom into a ClaimRow via the injected fetcher + evidence-locked
152	    judge. Every atom yields exactly one row (none silently excluded)."""
153	    rows: list[ClaimRow] = []
154	    for atom in atoms:
155	        ref = _most_specific_ref(atom)
156	        if ref is None:                                   # uncited
157	            jv = judge(atom.text, None, None)
158	            rows.append(_uncited_row(atom, jv))
159	            continue
160	        # an author-year/numbered citation that resolves to NO source
161	        if ref.kind != "ev_span" and not ref.resolved:
162	            jv = judge(atom.text, None, ref)
163	            rows.append(_unreachable_row(atom, ref, "source_missing", _severity(jv)))
164	            continue
165	        span = span_fetcher(ref)
166	        if span.text is None:                             # fetched but unreachable
167	            jv = judge(atom.text, None, ref)
168	            rows.append(_unreachable_row(
169	                atom, ref, span.unreachable_subtype or "fetch_failure", _severity(jv),
170	            ))
171	            continue
172	        jv = judge(atom.text, span.text, ref)
173	        rows.append(_cited_row(atom, ref, jv, span.text))
174	    return rows
175

1	"""Path-B benchmark run capture (I-safety-002b #925).
2	
3	Run-scoped capture of every LLM provider completion (all roles) + retrieval-backend
4	attempts, for the DR head-to-head gate. This module stores PLAIN DICTS; the runner
5	converts them to ``scripts.dr_benchmark.pathB_run_gate.LLMCall`` when calling
6	``assert_post_run`` — so this module has ZERO dependency on the gate / scripts package
7	and the production hot path never imports ``scripts`` from ``src``.
8	
9	Best-effort + gate-flagged: nothing is captured unless ``register_pathB_capture()`` has
10	been called for the current run, so when the benchmark gate is OFF the hot path pays only
11	one contextvar read. Capture NEVER raises (it must not break generation), mirroring the
12	I-gen-004 reasoning-sink pattern.
13	
14	Served-identity provenance: ``build_response_metadata`` reads ONLY genuinely-served fields
15	from the provider response (OpenRouter ``provider`` / served ``model`` / ``system_fingerprint``)
16	and DROPS missing fields. Request-derived values are never substituted, so a response that
17	fails to report its served provider/model makes the gate fail loud (in ``assert_post_run``)
18	rather than silently pass on request-derived data.
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import contextlib
24	import contextvars
25	import hashlib
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import threading
29	from pathlib import Path
30	from typing import Iterator
31	
32	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
33	
34	
35	def corpus_truncated_from_manifest(manifest: dict) -> bool:
36	    """#958 (S2): True iff a run's manifest records a truncated corpus (the
37	    post-fetch loop budget broke mid-corpus, leaving later candidates
38	    unclassified). Fail-safe False on absence/malformed input — a pre-#958
39	    manifest (no flag) is not falsely rejected; the flag is the positive signal
40	    a per-run consumer (Path-B gate + scorer) blocks on."""
41	    try:
42	        return bool(manifest.get("retrieval", {}).get("corpus_truncated", False))
43	    except Exception:
44	        return False
45	
46	
47	# Run-scoped collectors. The contextvars hold REFERENCES to a shared list/set; capture
48	# mutates them in place so appends inside asyncio child tasks (which copy the context by
49	# reference) remain visible at the top level where the runner reads them.
50	_SINK: contextvars.ContextVar[list | None] = contextvars.ContextVar("_PATHB_SINK", default=None)
51	_ROLE: contextvars.ContextVar[str | None] = contextvars.ContextVar("_PATHB_ROLE", default=None)
52	_RETRIEVAL: contextvars.ContextVar[set | None] = contextvars.ContextVar("_PATHB_RETRIEVAL", default=None)
53	# I-meta-002-q1d (#945): per-call retrieval trace (mirror of the generator's reasoning_trace.jsonl for the
54	# search/fetch half). Holds an ordered list of {kind: query|kept|drop, ...} records for the §-1.1 line-by-
55	# line audit. PURELY OBSERVATIONAL — populated by best-effort recorders that no-op when not started; the
56	# §9.1 retrieval/strict_verify chokepoint is never altered. Started fresh PER QUERY by start_retrieval_trace().
57	_RETRIEVAL_TRACE: contextvars.ContextVar[list | None] = contextvars.ContextVar(
58	    "_PATHB_RETRIEVAL_TRACE", default=None,
59	)
60	# I-obs-001 #1141 AC2: optional path for LIVE-APPEND of each retrieval record as it happens, so
61	# retrieval_trace.jsonl is `tail -f | jq`-safe DURING retrieval (today the file only materializes at
62	# the end-of-retrieval flush). None (the default; every no-path caller / unit test) => zero file I/O,
63	# in-memory list + artifacts byte-identical. The recorders run single-threaded on the main context
64	# (verified call sites); the lock is future-proofing only (ThreadPoolExecutor fetch workers do NOT
65	# inherit contextvars, so a stray worker call CV-gets None and no-ops).
66	_RETRIEVAL_TRACE_PATH: contextvars.ContextVar[Path | None] = contextvars.ContextVar(
67	    "_PATHB_RETRIEVAL_TRACE_PATH", default=None,
68	)
69	_RETRIEVAL_TRACE_LOCK = threading.Lock()
70	# I-bug-946 (#932): per-role resolved provider (e.g. {"generator":"Fireworks","evaluator":"Novita"}).
71	# Populated by gate_around_question() after preflight resolves each role's actual served provider
72	# via GET /api/v1/models/<id>/endpoints. openrouter_client and entailment_judge read this to force
73	# singleton provider routing in their request bodies (otherwise OpenRouter's silent fallback would
74	# defeat the strict-identity guarantee that smoke #15 caught: evaluator routed to Novita while pin
75	# expected Fireworks). Per Codex iter-2 P2: ContextVar lives in src (NOT scripts/) so the hot path
76	# never imports scripts. Codex APPROVE iter 2 on I-bug-946 brief.
77	_ROLE_PROVIDER: contextvars.ContextVar[dict | None] = contextvars.ContextVar(
78	    "_PATHB_ROLE_PROVIDER", default=None,
79	)
80	
81	
82	def register_pathB_capture() -> None:
83	    """Begin capture for the current run (fresh collectors). Call once at run start."""
84	    _SINK.set([])
85	    _RETRIEVAL.set(set())
86	
87	
88	def clear_pathB_capture() -> None:
89	    """End capture (gate off again). Call at run end."""
90	    _SINK.set(None)
91	    _ROLE.set(None)
92	    _RETRIEVAL.set(None)
93	    _RETRIEVAL_TRACE.set(None)
94	    _RETRIEVAL_TRACE_PATH.set(None)  # I-obs-001 #1141 AC2: never leak the live path past run end
95	    _ROLE_PROVIDER.set(None)
96	
97	
98	def set_role_providers(mapping: dict[str, str]):
99	    """I-bug-946 (#932): set the resolved per-role provider mapping for the gate run.
100	
101	    Called by gate_around_question() AFTER preflight() resolves each role's served provider
102	    via OpenRouter's /api/v1/models/<id>/endpoints. Returns a token; pair with
103	    reset_role_providers() in try/finally so the mapping never leaks beyond the gate scope.
104	    """
105	    return _ROLE_PROVIDER.set(mapping)
106	
107	
108	def reset_role_providers(token) -> None:
109	    _ROLE_PROVIDER.reset(token)
110	
111	
112	def current_role_provider() -> str | None:
113	    """Return the resolved provider for the CURRENT role (read via _ROLE contextvar).
114	
115	    Returns None if either the gate is off (mapping is None) or the current role has no
116	    entry in the mapping. openrouter_client calls this; when None it falls back to its
117	    current env-driven path (gate-off mode).
118	
119	    NOTE: entailment_judge must NOT use this — see get_role_provider() below.
120	    """
121	    mapping = _ROLE_PROVIDER.get()
122	    if mapping is None:
123	        return None
124	    role = _ROLE.get()
125	    if role is None:
126	        return None
127	    return mapping.get(role)
128	
129	
130	def get_role_provider(role: str) -> str | None:
131	    """Explicit-role lookup, NOT keyed off the ambient _ROLE contextvar.
132	
133	    I-bug-946 (#932) Codex iter-1 diff P1#2: the entailment judge posts the evaluator
134	    model but is INVOKED from within the generator's _ROLE scope (provenance verification
135	    fires during section generation). Using current_role_provider() would resolve to the
136	    generator's provider (Fireworks) for a Gemma post — Fireworks doesn't host Gemma →
137	    silent re-route. The judge must pass role="evaluator" explicitly.
138	    """
139	    mapping = _ROLE_PROVIDER.get()
140	    if mapping is None:
141	        return None
142	    return mapping.get(role)
143	
144	
145	def is_active() -> bool:
146	    return _SINK.get() is not None
147	
148	
149	@contextlib.contextmanager
150	def llm_role(role: str) -> Iterator[None]:
151	    """Scope the role tag for LLM calls made inside the block (token set + restore).
152	
153	    Use a context manager (not a sticky setter) so a role never leaks to later calls."""
154	    token = _ROLE.set(role)
155	    try:
156	        yield
157	    finally:
158	        _ROLE.reset(token)
159	
160	
161	def set_role(role: str):
162	    """Set the role tag and return a token to restore on. Pair with reset_role(token)
163	    inside a try/finally — equivalent guarantee to llm_role() ctx-mgr, used where
164	    re-indenting a large multi-line call block under `with` would be invasive."""
165	    return _ROLE.set(role)
166	
167	
168	def reset_role(token) -> None:
169	    _ROLE.reset(token)
170	
171	
172	def current_llm_role() -> str | None:
173	    return _ROLE.get()
174	
175	
176	def record_retrieval_attempt(backend: str) -> None:
177	    """Record that a required retrieval backend ('serper'|'semantic_scholar') was attempted."""
178	    backends = _RETRIEVAL.get()
179	    if backends is not None:
180	        backends.add(backend)
181	
182	
183	def attempted_backends() -> set:
184	    backends = _RETRIEVAL.get()
185	    return set(backends) if backends is not None else set()
186	
187	
188	# ── I-meta-002-q1d (#945): per-call retrieval trace (best-effort, no-op when not started) ──────────
189	def start_retrieval_trace(path: "Path | str | None" = None) -> None:
190	    """Begin a FRESH retrieval trace for the current query (P2 lifecycle hygiene, Codex brief-gate):
191	    a new list so a prior query's records can never leak into a later run_dir flush. Call once at the
192	    top of run_one_query, before retrieval.
193	
194	    I-obs-001 #1141 AC2: if ``path`` is given, each record is ALSO live-appended to it as it happens
195	    (tail -f safe). Default None => no file I/O (in-memory list only) so every no-path caller / unit
196	    test stays byte-unchanged."""
197	    _RETRIEVAL_TRACE.set([])
198	    _RETRIEVAL_TRACE_PATH.set(Path(path) if path else None)
199	
200	
201	def _live_append(rec: dict) -> None:
202	    """I-obs-001 #1141 AC2: best-effort APPEND of one record to the live retrieval_trace.jsonl.
203	
204	    No-op when no path was supplied to start_retrieval_trace (every unit test / non-benchmark caller).
205	    NEVER raises (module contract: capture never raises). Append mode keeps the file a valid JSONL
206	    prefix even on a mid-run kill; the end-of-retrieval ``"w"`` flush stays the reconciling final
207	    writer, so final content/order is byte-identical on every normal exit."""
208	    path = _RETRIEVAL_TRACE_PATH.get()
209	    if path is None:
210	        return
211	    try:
212	        with _RETRIEVAL_TRACE_LOCK:
213	            with open(path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
214	                fh.write(json.dumps(rec, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
215	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — capture NEVER raises (module contract, lines 11-12)
216	        logger.warning("retrieval_trace live-append failed", exc_info=True)
217	
218	
219	def record_retrieval_query(backend: str, query: str, urls: list[str]) -> None:
220	    """Record one search/fetch backend call: backend, query text, return count, returned URLs."""
221	    trace = _RETRIEVAL_TRACE.get()
222	    if trace is not None:
223	        rec = {
224	            "kind": "query", "backend": backend, "query": query,
225	            "return_count": len(urls), "urls": list(urls),
226	        }
227	        trace.append(rec)
228	        _live_append(rec)  # I-obs-001 #1141 AC2: same dict object as the in-memory record
229	
230	
231	def record_retrieval_kept(url: str, backend: str) -> None:
232	    """Record that a fetched source was KEPT into the evidence pool, with its originating backend."""
233	    trace = _RETRIEVAL_TRACE.get()
234	    if trace is not None:
235	        rec = {"kind": "kept", "url": url, "backend": backend}
236	        trace.append(rec)
237	        _live_append(rec)  # I-obs-001 #1141 AC2
238

241	    """Record that a candidate/source was DROPPED, with the reason (content_starved | fetch_failed |
242	    offtopic | rerank_not_selected | ...)."""
243	    trace = _RETRIEVAL_TRACE.get()
244	    if trace is not None:
245	        rec = {"kind": "drop", "url": url, "reason": reason}
246	        trace.append(rec)
247	        _live_append(rec)  # I-obs-001 #1141 AC2

251	    """Return the current query's retrieval-trace records (empty when not started)."""
252	    trace = _RETRIEVAL_TRACE.get()
253	    return list(trace) if trace is not None else []

257	    sink = _SINK.get()
258	    return list(sink) if sink is not None else []

261	def request_hash(messages) -> str:
262	    """Stable hash of the prompt messages (presence/integrity, not content storage)."""
263	    try:
264	        payload = json.dumps(messages, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False, default=str)
265	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
266	        payload = repr(messages)
267	    return hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()

269	
270	def build_response_metadata(raw_response: dict | None) -> dict:
271	    """Served-identity metadata from the provider response — PROVEN-present fields only.
272	
273	    provider_name <- raw['provider'] (OpenRouter), model <- served raw['model'],
274	    system_fingerprint <- raw['system_fingerprint']. None/missing fields are DROPPED
275	    (never request-filled), so an unreported served provider/model surfaces as a gate
276	    failure rather than a false pass.
277	
278	    Streaming responses synthesize ``data`` with a request-derived ``model`` fallback, so
279	    the client stashes the genuinely-SSE-served identity under ``_pathb_served``; when that
280	    key is present it is the authoritative source (request-derived ``data['model']`` is
281	    never counted as served).
282	
283	    Self-host endpoint (I-meta-002 PR-7/M1): a self-hosted vLLM verifier role (Mirror /
284	    Sentinel / Judge) carries NO OpenRouter ``provider`` field — its served identity for the
285	    M4 served==pinned check is the ENDPOINT it was served from. The transport stashes that
286	    endpoint under ``_pathb_served['endpoint']``; this function surfaces it as an OPTIONAL
287	    ``endpoint`` key. Backward-compatible: the key is DROPPED when ``_pathb_served`` does not
288	    carry it (existing OpenRouter 3-key behaviour is unchanged when absent)."""
289	    raw = raw_response or {}
290	    served = raw.get("_pathb_served")
291	    src = served if isinstance(served, dict) else raw
292	    meta = {
293	        "provider_name": src.get("provider"),
294	        "model": src.get("model"),
295	        "system_fingerprint": src.get("system_fingerprint"),
296	        "endpoint": src.get("endpoint"),
297	    }
298	    return {k: v for k, v in meta.items() if v is not None}
299	
300	
301	def capture_llm_call(*, role: str, messages, raw_response: dict | None) -> None:
302	    """Best-effort: append one captured provider completion. Never raises; no-op when off."""
303	    sink = _SINK.get()
304	    if sink is None:
305	        return
306	    try:
307	        sink.append(
308	            {
309	                "call_id": f"{role}-{len(sink)}",
310	                "role": role,
311	                "prompt_messages_present": bool(messages),
312	                "request_hash": request_hash(messages),
313	                "response_metadata": build_response_metadata(raw_response),
314	            }
315	        )
316	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — capture must never break generation
317	        logger.warning("[pathB] capture_llm_call failed (%s) — call not recorded", exc)

1	"""Path-B gate lifecycle helper for the honest_sweep runner (I-safety-002b #925).
2	
3	Wraps each benchmark question with the gate: preflight + register capture sink + run +
4	assert_post_run before any scoring. Persists the pin record to the run dir. Self-contained
5	so the runner diff is minimal (1 import + 1 ``with`` per question).
6

8	so the existing honest_sweep pipeline is untouched outside the benchmark.
9

11	served_metadata_provenance) and the Codex-APPROVED PR-2 role-tag fork (Option B: capture
12	only explicitly-tagged report-generator + report-evaluator calls; auxiliary scope/inductor
13	LLMs are not gated).
14	"""

17

19	import json
20	import logging
21	import os

23	from typing import Iterator

26	from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import validate_role_families
27	from src.polaris_graph.roles.openrouter_role_transport import benchmark_verifier_slug
28	from scripts.architecture.verify_lock import load_lock
29	from scripts.dr_benchmark.pathB_run_gate import (
30	    GateError,
31	    LLMCall,
32	    RolePin,
33	    assert_post_run,
34	    preflight,
35	)

37	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

39	# The four locked architecture roles (config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml).
40	# Order is generator -> mirror -> sentinel -> judge (the canonical pipeline order).
41	_LOCKED_ROLES = ("generator", "mirror", "sentinel", "judge")
42	
43	# Per-role env-var OVERRIDE knobs, applied ON TOP of the lock-sourced default slug. Mirrors
44	# config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml:env_vars. The generator additionally honors
45	# the legacy OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL fallback (documented honest_sweep override chain).
46	_ROLE_ENV_OVERRIDE = {
47	    "generator": "PG_GENERATOR_MODEL",
48	    "mirror": "PG_MIRROR_MODEL",
49	    "sentinel": "PG_SENTINEL_MODEL",
50	    "judge": "PG_JUDGE_MODEL",
51	}
52	
53	
54	def _benchmark_openrouter_route() -> bool:
55	    """True iff the four-role transport is the benchmark OpenRouter route (PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT
56	    unset or 'openrouter' — the DEFAULT). On that route the per-claim verifiers are served by the
57	    benchmark lineup over OpenRouter (the moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 Judge etc.), NOT the lock's sovereign
58	    self-host boxes. 'self_host' => the lock route (qwen Judge on the vast box), so the benchmark
59	    override below does NOT apply. Mirrors run_gate_b.four_role_transport_mode's default test WITHOUT
60	    importing the heavy benchmark CLI (LAW VII: a benchmark-side helper, not a lock change)."""
61	    return (os.getenv("PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT") or "openrouter").strip().lower() == "openrouter"
62	
63	
64	def _lock_default_slug(role: str) -> str:
65	    """The role's default model_slug, SOURCED FROM the architecture lock so pins + lock cannot
66	    drift (Codex P2, accepted). Reads lock['required_roles'][role]['model_slug']."""
67	    lock = load_lock()
68	    return str(lock["required_roles"][role]["model_slug"]).strip()
69	
70	
71	def _resolve_role_slug(role: str) -> str:
72	    """Resolve a role's effective slug: lock default, with the per-role PG_*_MODEL env override
73	    applied on top. The generator preserves its documented PG_GENERATOR_MODEL >
74	    OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL > lock-default precedence (regression: a sweep that exports only
75	    OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL must still pin the right generator)."""
76	    default = _lock_default_slug(role)
77	    if role == "generator":
78	        return (
79	            os.getenv("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL")
80	            or os.getenv("OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL")
81	            or default
82	        ).strip()
83	    env_name = _ROLE_ENV_OVERRIDE[role]
84	    return (os.getenv(env_name) or default).strip()
85	
86	
87	def _role_pins() -> list[RolePin]:
88	    """Build the FOUR per-role pins (generator/mirror/sentinel/judge) from the lock + env.
89	
90	    I-meta-002 sub-PR-5: the 2-role (generator+evaluator) pin set is replaced by the locked
91	    4-role set. The post-run gate enforces completeness in BOTH directions — every pinned role
92	    must be observed AND every observed call must be pinned (pathB_run_gate.assert_post_run
93	    lines 471/479) — so the pin set must EXACTLY match the captured roles. The 4-role pipeline
94	    emits generator/mirror/sentinel/judge calls; a leftover legacy 'evaluator' pin would
95	    therefore gate-FAIL at runtime (no evaluator call is captured). No production caller pins
96	    'evaluator' directly (only this function feeds preflight via gate_around_question), so the
97	    role is dropped here cleanly.
98	
99	    Default slugs are LOCK-SOURCED via load_lock() (Codex P2, accepted) so the pins and the
100	    architecture lock cannot drift; per-role PG_*_MODEL env vars override on top. The generator
101	    keeps its PG_GENERATOR_MODEL > OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL > lock-default precedence.
102	
103	    validate_role_families() runs on the effective 4-role map so the N-way family invariant
104	    (all 4 lineages distinct) holds at pin-build time — a same-family misconfiguration fails
105	    LOUD here rather than silently at runtime.
106	
107	    Codex PR-2 diff iter-1 P1 #2: surrogate_fields are ONLY those PROVEN present in the served
108	    response (provider_name + model). assert_post_run treats a missing surrogate field as
109	    fatal; system_fingerprint is recorded when present but is NOT a required surrogate.
110	
111	    I-bug-946 (#932): provider_name is left empty here; preflight() resolves the ACTUAL served
112	    provider per role via /api/v1/models/<id>/endpoints (the env is a candidate list)."""
113	    slug_by_role = {role: _resolve_role_slug(role) for role in _LOCKED_ROLES}
114	    # I-judge-kimi (2026-06-29): BENCHMARK-AWARE Judge pin. On the benchmark OpenRouter route the
115	    # Judge is NOT served by the lock's sovereign self-host qwen — qwen's ~2 OpenRouter providers
116	    # 429-tore the per-claim D8 seam, so the benchmark Judge was swapped to moonshotai/kimi-k2.6
117	    # (21 OpenRouter endpoints; see openrouter_role_transport._BENCHMARK_LINEUP_DEFAULT_SLUG). The
118	    # served identity the post-run gate must match is therefore the BENCHMARK lineup slug
119	    # (benchmark_verifier_slug('judge') = kimi) served OVER OpenRouter — NOT qwen @ vast_self_host_fp8.
120	    # So on this route the Judge pin's slug comes from the benchmark lineup AND its serving_route is
121	    # forced to 'openrouter' (set on the RolePin below; preflight preserves a pre-set route). The
122	    # Mirror/Sentinel/Generator pins are UNCHANGED. The SOVEREIGN lock path is untouched: in
123	    # self_host mode this override is OFF (the Judge stays the lock's qwen @ vast_self_host_fp8), and
124	    # PG_JUDGE_MODEL / the openrouter_client default stay the canonical-pinned qwen. This is a
125	    # benchmark-side serving override, NOT a lock change.
126	    judge_on_benchmark_route = _benchmark_openrouter_route()
127	    if judge_on_benchmark_route:
128	        slug_by_role["judge"] = benchmark_verifier_slug("judge")
129	    # N-way family segregation on the effective 4-role map (raises RuntimeError on collision).
130	    # I-beatboth-008 (#1285): honor the lock's family_policy.allowed_collisions (the single
131	    # source of truth) so the operator-approved all-GLM-5.2 generator+mirror collision PASSES
132	    # while a NON-listed same-family collision still RAISES. validate_role_families already
133	    # honors allowed_collisions under all_distinct (openrouter_client.py:774-787).
134	    _fp = load_lock().get("family_policy", {})
135	    _allowed_collisions = [tuple(str(x) for x in pair) for pair in _fp.get("allowed_collisions", [])]
136	    validate_role_families(slug_by_role, allowed_collisions=_allowed_collisions)
137	    surrogate_fields = ("provider_name", "model")
138	    return [
139	        RolePin(
140	            role,
141	            slug_by_role[role],
142	            "",
143	            surrogate_fields,
144	            # I-judge-kimi: the benchmark Judge is served over OpenRouter (kimi), so pin its route
145	            # to 'openrouter' to OVERRIDE the lock's vast_self_host_fp8 (preflight preserves a
146	            # pre-set serving_route). Every other role passes None -> preflight sources its route
147	            # from the lock as before (byte-identical: generator/mirror=openrouter, sentinel=
148	            # vast_self_host, judge=vast_self_host_fp8 in the sovereign self_host path).
149	            serving_route=("openrouter" if role == "judge" and judge_on_benchmark_route else None),
150	            # I-judge-kimi: the benchmark Judge is DELIBERATELY UNPINNED on OpenRouter (the
151	            # judge `order`/allow_fallbacks pin was removed from openrouter_provider_routing.yaml so
152	            # OpenRouter LOAD-BALANCES the ~178 per-claim D8 calls across kimi's 21 endpoints -> no
153	            # 429), and the blank-verdict recovery rotates providers. So a legitimate, fully-verified
154	            # kimi run serves MANY providers; allow_provider_drift tells assert_post_run to enforce
155	            # served==pinned MODEL (kimi) over the OpenRouter route but ALLOW the provider to vary.
156	            # ONLY the benchmark kimi Judge gets this; Mirror/Sentinel/Generator + every self-host
157	            # role keep allow_provider_drift=False (strict single-provider gate, byte-for-byte).
158	            allow_provider_drift=(role == "judge" and judge_on_benchmark_route),
159	        )
160	        for role in _LOCKED_ROLES
161	    ]
162	
163	
164	def role_tag(role: str):
165	    """Path-B capture role-tag context manager for a role-pipeline LLM call.
166	
167	    Thin re-export of pathB_capture.llm_role so the 4-role call sites (Mirror/Sentinel/Judge)
168	    can scope their served-identity capture with the correct role string WITHOUT each importing
169	    pathB_capture directly. pathB_capture already accepts arbitrary role strings via its _ROLE
170	    contextvar, so "mirror" / "sentinel" / "judge" are supported with no capture-side change.
171	    Use as `with role_tag("sentinel"): ...`. The deep sweep call-site wiring is sub-PR-6."""
172	    return _capture.llm_role(role)
173	
174	
175	def _salt() -> bytes:
176	    return (os.getenv("PG_PATHB_GATE_SALT") or "pathB-default-unsalted").encode("utf-8")
177	
178	
179	@contextlib.contextmanager
180	def gate_around_question(
181	    *, enabled: bool, run_dir: Path, control_vars: list[str] | None = None,
182	) -> Iterator[None]:
183	    """Wrap one benchmark question's run with preflight + post-run assertion.
184	
185	    Usage in the runner:
186	        with gate_around_question(enabled=args.pathB_gate, run_dir=run_dir):
187	            ... process_query body, including the role-tagged LLM calls ...
188	            # On normal exit, assert_post_run runs (raises GateError if not full-power).
189	
190	    Behavior:
191	    - enabled=False: no-op (yields immediately, no capture, no preflight).
192	    - enabled=True: preflight (raises GateError if not full-power); register_pathB_capture;
193	      yield; on normal exit, assert_post_run; persist pin + result to ``run_dir``. On
194	      exception inside the body, capture is cleared but the exception propagates.
195	    """
196	    if not enabled:
197	        yield
198	        return
199	
200	    pin_path = run_dir / "pathB_gate_pin.json"
201	    result_path = run_dir / "pathB_gate_result.json"
202	    invalid_sentinel = run_dir / "pathB_gate_INVALID"
203	    run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
204	
205	    # Codex PR-2 diff iter-1 P3: write a FAIL result file even when preflight raises,
206	    # so a missing-credential / unreachable-backend failure has an explicit per-run record
207	    # (otherwise the only artifact is a stderr exception, no traceable artifact).
208	    try:
209	        pin = preflight(
210	            control_vars=list(control_vars or []),
211	            role_pins=_role_pins(),
212	            salt=_salt(),
213	            roots=[Path("src/polaris_graph"), Path("scripts")],
214	            offline=False,  # real run = real reachability ping (gate enforce-by-default).
215	        )
216	    except GateError as exc:
217	        result_path.write_text(
218	            json.dumps(
219	                {"verdict": "FAIL", "stage": "preflight", "reason": str(exc)},
220	                indent=2, sort_keys=True,
221	            ),
222	            encoding="utf-8",
223	        )
224	        invalid_sentinel.write_text(
225	            f"preflight FAIL: {exc}\n", encoding="utf-8",
226	        )
227	        logger.error("[pathB] preflight FAIL: %s", exc)
228	        raise
229	    pin_path.write_text(json.dumps(pin, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str),
230	                        encoding="utf-8")
231	    _capture.register_pathB_capture()
232	    # I-bug-946 (#932): publish the resolved per-role provider mapping via ContextVar so
233	    # openrouter_client and entailment_judge force singleton routing in their request bodies.
234	    # Without this, OpenRouter's silent fallback re-routes mid-run and the post_run gate fails
235	    # (smoke #15: evaluator served by Novita while pin expected Fireworks).
236	    role_provider_map = {
237	        rp["role"]: rp["provider_name"]
238	        for rp in pin.get("role_pins", [])
239	        if rp.get("provider_name")
240	    }
241	    rp_token = _capture.set_role_providers(role_provider_map) if role_provider_map else None
242	    try:
243	        yield
244	    except Exception:
245	        # Propagate; do not run assert_post_run on a failed run (it would mask the cause).
246	        if rp_token is not None:
247	            _capture.reset_role_providers(rp_token)
248	        _capture.clear_pathB_capture()
249	        raise
250	
251	    try:
252	        # #958 (S2): a truncated corpus (post-fetch loop budget broke mid-corpus)
253	        # is a fail-loud non-clean signal — block PASS BEFORE the assert/PASS write.
254	        # Reuses the existing GateError handler below (→ FAIL result + INVALID sentinel).
255	        try:
256	            _manifest = json.loads(
257	                (run_dir / "manifest.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
258	            )
259	        except Exception:
260	            _manifest = {}
261	        if _capture.corpus_truncated_from_manifest(_manifest):
262	            _rt = _manifest.get("retrieval", {})
263	            raise GateError(
264	                "corpus truncated — post-fetch loop budget broke mid-corpus "
265	                f"({_rt.get('candidates_processed')}/{_rt.get('candidates_total')} "
266	                "candidates processed); run is partial, not full-power"
267	            )
268	        calls = [LLMCall(**c) for c in _capture.collected_calls()]
269	        backends = _capture.attempted_backends()
270	        result = assert_post_run(
271	            pin=pin,
272	            control_vars=list(control_vars or []),
273	            salt=_salt(),
274	            calls=calls,
275	            retrieval_backends_attempted=backends,
276	        )
277	        result_path.write_text(
278	            json.dumps({"verdict": "PASS", **result}, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str),
279	            encoding="utf-8",
280	        )
281	        logger.info("[pathB] gate PASS for run_dir=%s", run_dir)
282	    except GateError as exc:
283	        result_path.write_text(
284	            json.dumps(
285	                {"verdict": "FAIL", "stage": "post_run_assert", "reason": str(exc)},
286	                indent=2, sort_keys=True,
287	            ),
288	            encoding="utf-8",
289	        )
290	        # Codex PR-2 diff iter-1 P2 #2: write an INVALID sentinel so downstream scoring
291	        # (PR-3 claim_audit_scorer) can skip the run_dir's per-run artifacts even though
292	        # they were written during the run (manifest/judge/etc.). The gate is the source
293	        # of truth for run validity; the sentinel makes that machine-readable.
294	        invalid_sentinel.write_text(
295	            f"post-run gate FAIL: {exc}\n", encoding="utf-8",
296	        )
297	        logger.error("[pathB] gate FAIL for run_dir=%s: %s", run_dir, exc)
298	        raise
299	    finally:
300	        _capture.clear_pathB_capture()
301

File names `pathB_capture.py` and `pathB_runner.py` use mixed-case "pathB" — inconsistent with Python snake_case convention (should be `path_b_capture.py`). This is a naming-convention issue but not really "unprofessional" per the marketing/joke/temporal categories — it's a project-internal codename ("Path B" as in Plan A/Plan B) baked into a filename. I'll flag as a mild rename candidate for convention (not primary target of this audit's categories, but "Path B"/ "Path-B" as a vague/informal name that doesn't describe what the module does could qualify). Given strict bar instruction, I'll flag it.

42	    intake_status: str | None = None
43	    intake_scope_class: str | None = None
44	    intake_decision: dict[str, Any] | None = None  # raw ScopeDecision dict
45	    evidence_pool: dict[str, Any] | None = None    # raw EvidencePool dict
46	    verified_report: dict[str, Any] | None = None  # raw VerifiedReport dict
47	    intake_latency_ms: int = 0
48	    retrieval_latency_ms: int = 0
49	    generation_latency_ms: int = 0
50	    total_latency_ms: int = 0
51	    bundle_available: bool = False
52	    failure: str | None = None  # populated iff something failed; chain stopped
53	
54	    def succeeded(self) -> bool:
55	        return self.failure is None
56	
57	
58	def run_polaris_against(
59	    config: BenchmarkConfig,
60	    backend_url: str,
61	    *,
62	    client: httpx.Client | None = None,
63	    skip_generation_for_bait: bool = True,
64	) -> dict[str, PolarisRunResult]:
65	    """Run every BenchmarkQuestion through the live POLARIS chain.
66	
67	    Args:
68	        config: BenchmarkConfig with questions
69	        backend_url: e.g. 'http://127.0.0.1:8000' (no trailing /)
70	        client: optional pre-configured httpx.Client (tests inject MockTransport)
71	        skip_generation_for_bait: when True, refusal-bait questions stop
72	            after intake (since we expect them to refuse + scoring needs
73	            only the intake_status). Set False to forcibly attempt
74	            retrieval/generation on bait (will likely 400).
75	
76	    Returns:
77	        dict[question_id -> PolarisRunResult]. Includes failure rows.
78	    """
79	    out: dict[str, PolarisRunResult] = {}
80	    own_client = client is None
81	    if own_client:
82	        client = httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S)
83	
84	    try:
85	        for question in config.questions:
86	            out[question.question_id] = _run_one(
87	                question, backend_url, client, skip_generation_for_bait
88	            )
89	    finally:
90	        if own_client and client is not None:
91	            client.close()
92	
93	    return out
94	
95	
96	def _run_one(
97	    question: BenchmarkQuestion,
98	    backend_url: str,
99	    client: httpx.Client,
100	    skip_generation_for_bait: bool,
101	) -> PolarisRunResult:
102	    result = PolarisRunResult(question_id=question.question_id)
103	    t_start = time.perf_counter()
104	
105	    # 1. Intake
106	    try:
107	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
108	        r = client.post(
109	            f"{backend_url}/api/intake",
110	            json={"question": question.question_text},
111	        )
112	        result.intake_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
113	        if r.status_code != 200:
114	            result.failure = f"intake HTTP {r.status_code}: {r.text[:200]}"
115	            return result
116	        intake_body = r.json()
117	        decision = intake_body["decision"]
118	        result.intake_decision = decision
119	        result.intake_status = decision.get("status")
120	        result.intake_scope_class = decision.get("scope_class")
121	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
122	        result.failure = f"intake exception {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
123	        return result
124	
125	    # Refusal-bait: stop here — we have what scoring needs
126	    if question.is_refusal_bait and skip_generation_for_bait:
127	        result.total_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t_start) * 1000)
128	        return result
129	
130	    # If intake said NOT in_scope but the question wasn't bait, it's a
131	    # genuine failure of the question (or a slice 001 false positive).
132	    # Stop with the intake info captured.
133	    if result.intake_status != "in_scope":
134	        result.total_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t_start) * 1000)
135	        return result
136	
137	    # 2. Retrieval
138	    try:
139	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
140	        r = client.post(
141	            f"{backend_url}/api/retrieval",
142	            json={"decision": decision},
143	        )
144	        result.retrieval_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
145	        if r.status_code != 200:
146	            result.failure = f"retrieval HTTP {r.status_code}: {r.text[:200]}"
147	            return result
148	        retrieval_body = r.json()
149	        result.evidence_pool = retrieval_body["pool"]
150	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
151	        result.failure = f"retrieval exception {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
152	        return result
153	
154	    # Inadequate corpus -> can't generate
155	    if not result.evidence_pool["adequacy"]["is_adequate"]:
156	        result.total_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t_start) * 1000)
157	        return result
158	
159	    # 3. Generation
160	    try:
161	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
162	        r = client.post(
163	            f"{backend_url}/api/generation",
164	            json={
165	                "pool": result.evidence_pool,
166	                "scope_class": result.intake_scope_class,
167	            },
168	        )
169	        result.generation_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
170	        if r.status_code != 200:
171	            result.failure = f"generation HTTP {r.status_code}: {r.text[:200]}"
172	            return result
173	        gen_body = r.json()
174	        result.verified_report = gen_body["report"]
175	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
176	        result.failure = f"generation exception {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
177	        return result
178	
179	    # 4. Bundle availability check (HEAD or attempt; cheap)
180	    if (
181	        result.verified_report
182	        and result.verified_report.get("pipeline_verdict") == "success"
183	    ):
184	        try:
185	            r = client.get(f"{backend_url}/api/audit-bundle/health")
186	            result.bundle_available = r.status_code == 200
187	        except Exception:
188	            result.bundle_available = False
189	
190	    result.total_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t_start) * 1000)
191	    return result
192

1	"""I-meta-006 (#1006) — system-agnostic ATOMIC claim + citation extraction.
2	
3	Turns ANY deep-research report (POLARIS, ChatGPT, Gemini) into a uniform list of
4	ATOMIC factual claims, each carrying the citation(s) it attaches to, so the SAME
5	§-1.1 faithfulness audit runs identically across systems (Codex design-gate
6	APPROVE iter3). It assigns NO verdicts and confers NO auto-pass — POLARIS
7	`[#ev:]` spans are used for extraction + to DEFINE the cited span, never to
8	auto-verify.
9	
10	Cash-free + deterministic given the INJECTED ``atomizer`` (the real atomizer runs
11	in the operator-gated paid run; tests pass a deterministic fake). Compound
12	sentences split into separate factual atoms so the denominator is not
13	methodology-dependent (Codex iter-1 P1-2). An atom with NO citation is KEPT (it
14	scores as uncited downstream — Codex iter-1 P2-1).
15	
16	Citation formats handled:
17	  - POLARIS: ``[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]`` (the exact span) + bare ``[N]`` →
18	    bibliography; reuses ``provenance_generator.parse_provenance_tokens``.
19	  - ChatGPT / Gemini: academic ``(Author, Year)`` keys + numbered superscripts,
20	    resolved against the report's reference list.
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import re
25	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
26	from typing import Callable
27	
28	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
29	    parse_provenance_tokens,
30	    split_into_sentences,
31	)
32	
33	# A system identifier.
34	SYSTEMS = ("polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini")
35	
36	# academic "(Author, 2015)" / "(Acemoglu & Restrepo, 2018, 2019)" inline keys.
37	_AUTHOR_YEAR_RE = re.compile(r"\(([^()]*?\b(?:19|20)\d{2}[a-z]?[^()]*?)\)")
38	# bare numbered citation markers: [1], [12].
39	_NUMBERED_RE = re.compile(r"\[(\d{1,3})\]")
40	# unicode superscript citation markers (Gemini-style), e.g. "churn²⁷".
41	_SUPERSCRIPT_RE = re.compile(r"[⁰¹²³⁴-⁹]+")
42	_SUPERSCRIPT_MAP = {
43	    "⁰": "0", "¹": "1", "²": "2", "³": "3", "⁴": "4",
44	    "⁵": "5", "⁶": "6", "⁷": "7", "⁸": "8", "⁹": "9",
45	}
46	# strip POLARIS provenance + bare numbered markers from the rendered atom text.
47	_POLARIS_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[A-Za-z0-9_]+:\d+-\d+\]")
48	_ATOM_HAS_ALPHA_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z]")
49	
50	
51	def _superscript_to_number(run: str) -> str:
52	    return "".join(_SUPERSCRIPT_MAP.get(c, "") for c in run)
53	
54	
55	@dataclass(frozen=True)
56	class CitationRef:
57	    """One citation a claim attaches to. ``kind='uncited'`` = no citation."""
58	    system: str
59	    kind: str                 # "ev_span" | "author_year" | "numbered" | "uncited"
60	    raw_key: str              # the marker text as it appeared in the report
61	    resolved: str | None = None   # url / evidence_id / reference text (None = unresolved)
62	    ev_start: int | None = None   # POLARIS [#ev:] span (the MOST SPECIFIC cited anchor)
63	    ev_end: int | None = None
64	
65	
66	@dataclass
67	class ExtractedAtom:
68	    atom_id: str
69	    text: str                       # clean prose, citation markers stripped
70	    citation_refs: list[CitationRef] = field(default_factory=list)
71	
72	    @property
73	    def is_cited(self) -> bool:
74	        return any(c.kind != "uncited" for c in self.citation_refs)
75	
76	
77	def _default_atomizer(sentence: str) -> list[str]:
78	    """Conservative deterministic fallback: split independent clauses on a
79	    semicolon. The real run injects a faithful atomizer; this keeps the harness
80	    deterministic + cash-free for the obvious compound case without inventing
81	    atoms."""
82	    parts = [p.strip() for p in sentence.split(";")]
83	    return [p for p in parts if p]
84	
85	
86	def _strip_markers(text: str, system: str) -> str:
87	    s = _POLARIS_TOKEN_RE.sub("", text)
88	    s = _NUMBERED_RE.sub("", s)
89	    if system != "polaris":
90	        s = _AUTHOR_YEAR_RE.sub("", s)
91	        s = _SUPERSCRIPT_RE.sub("", s)
92	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
93	
94	
95	def _extract_citations(
96	    sentence: str, system: str, references: dict[str, str],
97	) -> list[CitationRef]:
98	    refs: list[CitationRef] = []
99	    if system == "polaris":
100	        for tok in parse_provenance_tokens(sentence):
101	            refs.append(CitationRef(
102	                system=system, kind="ev_span", raw_key=tok.raw,
103	                resolved=tok.evidence_id, ev_start=tok.start, ev_end=tok.end,
104	            ))
105	        for m in _NUMBERED_RE.finditer(sentence):
106	            num = m.group(1)
107	            refs.append(CitationRef(
108	                system=system, kind="numbered", raw_key=m.group(0),
109	                resolved=references.get(num),
110	            ))
111	    else:
112	        for m in _AUTHOR_YEAR_RE.finditer(sentence):
113	            key = m.group(1).strip()
114	            refs.append(CitationRef(
115	                system=system, kind="author_year", raw_key=m.group(0),
116	                resolved=references.get(key) or references.get(_norm_key(key)),
117	            ))
118	        for m in _NUMBERED_RE.finditer(sentence):
119	            num = m.group(1)
120	            refs.append(CitationRef(
121	                system=system, kind="numbered", raw_key=m.group(0),
122	                resolved=references.get(num),
123	            ))
124	        for m in _SUPERSCRIPT_RE.finditer(sentence):
125	            num = _superscript_to_number(m.group(0))
126	            if num:
127	                refs.append(CitationRef(
128	                    system=system, kind="numbered", raw_key=m.group(0),
129	                    resolved=references.get(num),
130	                ))
131	    if not refs:
132	        refs.append(CitationRef(system=system, kind="uncited", raw_key=""))
133	    return refs
134	
135	
136	def _norm_key(key: str) -> str:
137	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", key.strip().lower())
138	
139	
140	def extract_atoms(
141	    report_text: str,
142	    system: str,
143	    references: dict[str, str] | None = None,
144	    *,
145	    atomizer: Callable[[str], list[str]] | None = None,
146	    question_id: str = "",
147	) -> list[ExtractedAtom]:
148	    """Extract atomic claims + their citations from a report. ``references`` maps
149	    a citation key (numbered string / author-year / normalized author-year) to a
150	    resolved source (url/title); ``atomizer`` is the INJECTED faithful atomizer
151	    (default = conservative semicolon split)."""
152	    if system not in SYSTEMS:
153	        raise ValueError(f"unknown system {system!r}; expected one of {SYSTEMS}")
154	    references = references or {}
155	    atomize = atomizer or _default_atomizer
156	
157	    atoms: list[ExtractedAtom] = []
158	    for si, sentence in enumerate(split_into_sentences(report_text)):
159	        refs = _extract_citations(sentence, system, references)
160	        for ai, raw_atom in enumerate(atomize(sentence)):
161	            text = _strip_markers(raw_atom, system)
162	            if not text or not _ATOM_HAS_ALPHA_RE.search(text):
163	                continue
164	            prefix = f"{system}:{question_id}:" if question_id else f"{system}:"
165	            atoms.append(ExtractedAtom(
166	                atom_id=f"{prefix}{si}:{ai}", text=text, citation_refs=list(refs),
167	            ))
168	    return atoms
169
